_comment: State Tax Treatment of Retirement Income — State income tax treatment of federal retirement income, property tax exemptions, and veteran-specific benefits by state. Version 2.1. Expand with additional states as needed. Data is general guidance — user should verify current state law.
_disclaimer: State tax law changes frequently. This file provides general guidance for planning purposes only. Users should verify current rules with their state tax authority or a qualified tax advisor.
_retiree_guidance: Military retirees should reference both 'income_tax' (retirement pay treatment, Social Security, tax brackets) and 'veteran_benefits' (property tax exemptions, disability-based benefits) for a complete state-level picture. The two sections are complementary — 'income_tax' covers all retirement income regardless of VA status, while 'veteran_benefits' covers VA disability-related property and housing exemptions.

income_tax.note: Alaska has no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Senior Citizen Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: First $150,000 of assessed value — mandatory statewide minimum
exemptions.note: Mandatory under AS 29.45.030(e). Municipalities may increase above $150,000.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Alaska. No state income tax. Benefits administered at municipal level with statewide minimum. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Mandatory exemption of first $150,000 of assessed value on primary residence for qualifying disabled veterans. Municipalities may enact additional exemptions above this amount.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.exemption_amount_note: Statewide mandatory minimum under AS 29.45.030(e). Some municipalities offer additional optional exemptions under AS 29.45.050.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.municipal_optional_note: Example: Matanuska-Susitna Borough offers additional $129,720 optional exemption on top of $150,000 mandatory

eligibility.rating_note: Service-connected disability rated 50% or more by VA or branch of service
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Statute references disability 'rated as 50 percent or more' — IU not explicitly addressed; recommend verification with borough assessor
eligibility.honorable_note: Discharge must be 'under a condition that is not dishonorable'
eligibility.residency_note: Must be AK resident for entire year prior to exemption year

occupancy_requirements.ownership_date: Must own and occupy before January 1 of assessment year
occupancy_requirements.days_note: Subsequent years require minimum 185 days occupancy as primary residence
occupancy_requirements.no_other_note: Cannot own other property receiving residential, senior citizen, or disabled veteran exemption

documentation: Current VA disability rating letter (dated within one year)
Trust documentation if property is in a trust

survivor_conditions.age_note: Widow/widower must be at least 60 years of age at time of veteran's death

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Mandatory exemption on first $150,000 of assessed value for veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability (AS 29.45.030(e)). Some municipalities offer additional optional amounts.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Exemption on first $150,000 of assessed value for veterans with 50%+ service-connected disability.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is exempt from Alabama income tax (Ala. Code 40-18-20)

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Alabama does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates from 2% to 5%

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption (Age 65+ or P&T Disabled)
exemptions.eligibility: Alabama resident age 65+ or permanently and totally disabled (regardless of age)
exemptions.income_limit_note: No income limit for P&T disabled; income limits apply for age-only qualification
exemptions.note: Exempt from all state, county, and city ad valorem property taxes on homestead and adjacent land up to 160 acres

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Alabama. P&T disabled veterans qualify for full homestead exemption (same as age 65+ exemption but with no income limit). SAH grant homes separately exempt. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Full exemption from all state, county, and city property taxes on homestead (primary residence and up to 160 acres). P&T disabled veterans qualify at any age with no income limit.

eligibility.rating_note: Must be permanently and totally disabled — Alabama uses general P&T standard, not specific VA rating percentage
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Verify with county — statute references permanent and total disability

application.note: Must own and occupy as primary residence on first day of tax year

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.survivor_note: General homestead exemption does not automatically transfer to surviving spouse — spouse must independently qualify (e.g., by age 65+)

sah_housing_exemption.description: Home acquired through VA Specially Adapted Housing grant is fully exempt from all Alabama property taxes regardless of value

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from license fees and ad valorem (property) taxes on one motor vehicle paid for fully or partially by VA funds to accommodate veteran's disability
vehicle_tax_exemption.max_vehicles_note: One disabled veteran license plate exempt from registration fees and taxes. Additional plates incur regular fees.

eligibility.va_funded_note: Vehicle must be all or partly paid for with VA funds to accommodate veteran's service-connected disability
eligibility.wartime_note: Veteran must have been disabled during any U.S. war, conflict, or period of hostilities against a foreign nation

registration_fee_exemption.description: Veterans with 10% or higher disability rating: first license plate exempt from registration fees. Veterans with 50%+ disability or special mechanical controls: $5.00 annual fee.
registration_fee_exemption.note: Registration fee exemption is separate from the VA-funded vehicle ad valorem exemption and available to a broader group

vehicle_tax_exemption.survivor_note: Surviving spouse may not order or retain disabled veteran license plate

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption from ad valorem property taxes on primary residence (<=160 acres, single-family) for 100% disabled veterans. Also available to veterans age 65+.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence (≤160 acres) for 100% disabled veterans.

military_retirement.note: Arkansas fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Arkansas does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Top rate 3.9% on income over $24,300 (2025). Rate has been gradually reducing.

exemptions.name: Homestead Tax Credit
exemptions.note: $375 annual property tax credit on homestead

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Arkansas veteran-specific benefits. Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans and veterans with specific severe injuries. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes on homestead and qualifying personal property for 100% disabled veterans, veterans who are blind, have lost use of a limb, or lost an eye

alternative_qualifying_conditions: Loss of use of one or more limbs

veteran_benefits._vehicle_note: Arkansas levies ad valorem tax on motor vehicles. The disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption covers both homestead and qualifying personal property including vehicles for 100% disabled veterans. Vehicle property tax is paid at time of annual tag renewal per ACA §26-26-1119.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption on homestead for veterans with 100% service-connected disability (blind, missing limb, or 100% disabled) under Ark. Code Sec. 26-3-306.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on homestead for veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

states._territory_note: Unincorporated US territory. American Samoa has sought to mirror its tax code on the federal IRC, but progress to fully incorporate federal tax benefits is unclear. Residents are US nationals (not citizens by birth) unless born to a US citizen parent.

income_tax._tax_system: Attempting mirror code adoption of federal IRC; implementation status unclear

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay tax treatment under American Samoa's tax code is unclear due to incomplete mirror code implementation

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt

ss_income.note: Social Security coverage applies; tax treatment under AS code unclear

income_tax.note: American Samoa's tax system is based on an incomplete adoption of the federal IRC. Residents should consult the American Samoa Tax Office for current treatment of retirement income.

property_tax.note: No specific veteran property tax exemptions have been identified for American Samoa

veteran_benefits._schema_note: American Samoa offers zero territory-level veteran benefits per CNAS analysis. Federal VA benefits (compensation, healthcare, education) apply. No territory-specific property tax or income tax exemptions have been identified.
veteran_benefits._no_benefits_note: As of 2026, American Samoa does not offer territory-specific veteran tax exemptions, property tax exemptions, or employment preference benefits. Veterans rely on federal VA benefits.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: American Samoa: No statewide veteran-specific property tax exemption identified. Contact American Samoa Tax Office.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: No statewide veteran-specific property tax exemption identified for American Samoa.

military_retirement.note: Arizona fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Arizona does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Arizona flat tax of 2.5% effective 2023

exemptions.name: Widow/Widower/Disabled Person Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: CPI-indexed annually. Assessed value cap of $36,865 applies to non-veteran disabled/widow(er) applicants.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Arizona. Major expansion effective January 1, 2026 via SB 1749 (Chapter 247). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_full_exemption.description: Full exemption from all property taxes on primary residence for veterans with 100% service-connected disability

eligibility.rating_note: Based on combined service-connected disability rating per VA, NOT compensation level
eligibility.iu_note: CRITICAL: IU veterans with combined ratings below 100% do NOT qualify for the full exemption. Maricopa County Assessor confirms combined service-connected rating is the controlling factor.

excluded_from_income: Military pensions (all types — expanded by SB 1749 from disability pensions only)
income_requirements.note: Income limits apply even for 100% service-connected full exemption

ownership_requirements.spouse_note: Property jointly owned with spouse is treated as if owned solely by the veteran for full exemption purposes

application.window: First Monday in January through March 1
documentation: VA Benefits Summary Letter showing 100% service-connected disability

survivor_conditions.property_note: Surviving spouse exemption applies to the property itself — if spouse sells, exemption ends
survivor_conditions.effective_note: Surviving spouse can first claim continued exemption beginning January 2027, only if veteran received full exemption in 2026 and passed away during 2026

disabled_veteran_full_exemption.authority: ARS § 42-11111 as amended by SB 1749 (Laws 2025, Chapter 247, signed June 27, 2025)

disabled_veteran_partial_exemption.description: Assessed value reduction prorated by disability percentage for veterans with service-connected or nonservice-connected disability
disabled_veteran_partial_exemption.exemption_amount_note: CPI-indexed annually. Amount is multiplied by veteran's disability percentage.

property_value_limit.note: Property assessment cap removed for veterans by SB 1749 (still applies to non-veteran disabled/widow(er) applicants)

application.window: First Monday in January through March 1

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Income and property value tested (~$4,188 assessed value for FY2025/2026, adjusted annually). Any total disability qualifies — not P&T specific.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Income and property-value tested partial exemption (~$4,188 assessed value) for totally disabled veterans.

military_retirement.note: California exempts up to $20,000 of military retired pay or SBP annuities from state income tax for qualifying taxpayers. Enacted June 27, 2025 (SB 132). Sunsets before tax year 2030 unless renewed.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: California does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates from 1% to 13.3% (highest in nation)

exemptions.name: Homeowners Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: $7,000 reduction in assessed value for owner-occupied primary residence
exemptions.note: Available to all homeowners; filed once with county assessor

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for California. Two-tier disabled veteran exemption indexed annually for inflation. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veterans_exemption.description: Reduces assessed value of primary residence for veterans rated 100% disabled or compensated at 100% due to IU. Two tiers: basic (no income test) and low-income (annual income verification).
disabled_veterans_exemption.inflation_note: Both exemption amounts and income limit compounded annually by BOE inflation factor

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans compensated at 100% rate due to individual unemployability qualify
eligibility.alternate_criteria: Blind in both eyes or loss of use of two or more limbs
eligibility.wartime_note: Must have served during designated period per R&TC 205.5(b)(1)(A)

application.deadline: By December 31 of the year exemption is sought for full amount
application.late_filing: 85% of exemption for first late year; retroactive filings up to 8 years

survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouse of qualified veteran or veteran who died of service-connected cause

veterans_exemption.description: General veterans exemption of $4,000 from assessed value for qualifying veterans with limited property
veterans_exemption.note: Very limited eligibility due to low property ownership caps. Most homeowners exceed the limit.

sb_296.would_do: Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans on primary residence, effective Jan 1, 2026 through Jan 1, 2036
sb_296.note: Bipartisan support; passed Senate 38-0 before being placed in Assembly suspense

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: 2026 amounts (inflation-indexed): Basic exemption $180,671 of assessed value; Low-income exemption $271,009 (household income <=81,131). One-time filing; continues automatically. Revenue and Taxation Code Sec. 205.5.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Partial assessed value exemption: $180,671 basic / $271,009 low-income (2026, inflation-indexed) for 100% disabled or IU veterans.

military_retirement.note: Colorado allows subtraction of military retirement pay from taxable income with age-based limits: up to $15,000 (under 55), $20,000 (55-64), or $24,000 (65+). These are part of the general pension/annuity subtraction. No special military-only exemption statute.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Colorado allows age-based subtraction of Social Security benefits: up to $15,000 (under 55), $20,000 (55-64), $24,000 (65+). Part of general retirement income subtraction. Flat tax rate 4.4%. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

income_tax.note: Colorado has a flat income tax rate of 4.4% (2024)

exemptions.name: Senior Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual value for seniors 65+ who have owned and occupied home for 10+ years

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exemptions for Colorado. Applications submitted to county assessor. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual value of primary residence for qualifying disabled veterans
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.max_tax_savings_note: Maximum exemption is 50% of $200,000 = $100,000 reduction in taxable value

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans with Individual Unemployability (TDIU) status qualify if rated at least 70% but compensated at 100%
eligibility.service_requirement: 24 months active duty (including National Guard called to active service)
eligibility.dhs_note: 100% permanent disability through DHS or military branch disability retirement also qualifies

residency_requirements.occupancy_note: Must live in property at least 51% of the year and pay Colorado resident taxes

application.file_with: County Assessor where property is located
documentation: State application form (from DOLA Property Tax Division or county assessor)
VA Benefit Summary Letter (Award Letter) showing 100% P&T status
Letter should be dated within last 365 days
application.renewal: Not required annually unless moved or VA status changes; must reapply at new address

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.state_reimbursement_note: State reimburses county treasurer for lost revenue

pending_initiative.initiative: 2025-2026 #49 (Property Tax Exemption for 100% P&T Disabled Veterans)
pending_initiative.proposed_change: Full (100%) property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T or TDIU veterans
pending_initiative.note: Current partial exemption would be replaced with full exemption if passed

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.authority: Colorado Constitution Article X, Section 3.5; Colorado Revised Statutes

gold_star_spouse_exemption.description: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual value for Gold Star spouses

eligibility.condition: Surviving spouse of service member who died in the line of duty
eligibility.documentation: Death gratuity from Department of Defense, or DD Form 1300 (Report of Casualty)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of actual home value for 100% P&T or IU veterans (max $100,000 value reduction). File by July 1 with CO DMVA.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: 50% exemption on first $200,000 of home value for 100% P&T or IU veterans (max $100,000 value reduction).

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from Connecticut income tax if taxed on federal return

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation and military disability retirement pay conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Connecticut exempts Social Security for single filers with AGI under $75,000 and joint filers under $100,000. Above those thresholds, no more than 25% of benefits are taxed. Tax rates range 2% to 6.99%. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 6.99% on income over $500,000 (2025)

exemptions.name: Wartime Veteran Basic Exemption
exemptions.eligibility: Honorably discharged veteran with 90+ days wartime service. Amount increases with municipality revaluation increase factors.
exemptions.note: Applied to assessed value of real estate or motor vehicle. Town-administered; actual amounts vary by municipality.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Connecticut veteran-specific property tax benefits. New (Oct 2024) full dwelling exemption for 100% P&T veterans. Older tiered system with base and disability add-ons. Amounts vary significantly by municipality due to revaluation increase factors. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

pt_disabled_veteran_dwelling_exemption.description: Full property tax exemption on dwelling (primary residence) for veterans with 100% permanent and total service-connected disability. Effective for Grand List years starting October 1, 2024. First reflected on 2026 tax bills.
pt_disabled_veteran_dwelling_exemption.coverage: Dwelling only (fractional share if co-owned). Law does not explicitly cover the lot/land. If veteran does not own a dwelling, one motor vehicle exempt instead.

eligibility.iu_eligible: Ambiguous — law references '100% permanent and total' rating but does not define whether IU/TDIU qualifies. OLR report notes the law is silent on this question.
eligibility.honorable_discharge_note: 100% disabled veterans exempt from the 90-day wartime service requirement

application.filing_deadline: Annually before January 1 for prior October 1 Grand List
application.late_filing: Law explicitly allows late filing of P&T disability proof

pt_disabled_veteran_dwelling_exemption.cannot_combine_with: Other veteran property tax exemptions under CGS §12-81(20)/(21)

disabled_veteran_tiered_exemption.description: Tiered exemption based on disability rating (10%+). Amounts vary by rating, income, and municipality revaluation increase factor. Example: 100% disabled veteran with income ≤$18,000 single/$21,000 married receives at least $10,515 exemption (base, before increase factor).

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Full property tax exemption on one motor vehicle for veterans with 100% P&T service-connected disability who do not own a dwelling. Connecticut levies annual municipal property tax on motor vehicles.

eligibility.must_not_own_dwelling_note: Vehicle exemption is alternative to dwelling exemption under PA 24-46. Veterans who own a dwelling receive the dwelling exemption instead.
eligibility.honorable_discharge_note: 100% P&T veterans exempt from 90-day wartime service requirement

vehicle_tax_exemption.effective_note: First reflected on 2026 tax bills (based on October 1, 2024 Grand List)

wartime_veteran_alternative.description: Wartime veterans (90+ days service) without real property may apply standard $1,000-$1,500 exemption toward motor vehicle assessment
wartime_veteran_alternative.income_qualified_amount_note: Income-qualified wartime veterans may receive higher exemption amounts set by municipality

active_duty_exemption.description: Active-duty CT residents receive total exemption on one CT-registered motor vehicle as of October 1 assessment date
active_duty_exemption.filing_deadline: December 31 of the year taxes are due

vehicle_tax_exemption.spouse_during_life_note: Vehicle belonging to spouse domiciled with P&T veteran is also covered
vehicle_tax_exemption.cannot_combine_with: Other veteran property tax exemptions under CGS §12-81(20)/(21)

application.form: Form D-2 (for P&T exemption); affidavit (for motor vehicle/mobile home)
application.filing_deadline: January 1 for prior October 1 Grand List

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Public Act 24-46 (effective October 1, 2024): Full exemption on primary residence dwelling for 100% P&T veterans. First reflected on 2026 tax bills. Town assessor filing required.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence dwelling for 100% P&T veterans (effective 2024).

ss_income.note: District of Columbia does not tax Social Security benefits

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable

military_retirement.note: DC residents pay tax on military retirement pay

dic.note: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) is tax-free

sbp.note: Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) annuities are excluded from DC gross income

income_tax.note: DC has graduated income tax rates up to 9.75%

exemptions.name: Homestead Deduction
exemptions.amount_note: Standard homestead deduction for 2026 — reduces assessed value
exemptions.note: Basic homestead deduction available to all owner-occupied primary residences

exemptions.name: Senior Citizen/Disabled Tax Relief
exemptions.note: Income limits apply

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for District of Columbia. All require documentation from VA. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

veterans_homestead_deduction.description: Large assessed value deduction for 100% P&T disabled veterans — reduces assessed value before computing property tax
veterans_homestead_deduction.deduction_amount_note: Amount may be adjusted annually — verify with DC OTR for current tax year

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans paid at 100% disability rating level due to Individual Unemployability (IU) also qualify
eligibility.ownership_note: Veteran must maintain at least 50% ownership of property as shown by deed
eligibility.domicile_note: Veteran must be domiciled in DC — the property must be principal residence
eligibility.dwelling_units_note: Property must have no more than 5 dwelling units

income_limit.note: Total household federal AGI cannot exceed the Senior/Disabled Tax Relief income limit for the applicable tax year

veterans_homestead_deduction.replaces_note: Properties receiving Veterans' Homestead Deduction are NOT eligible for standard Homestead Deduction, Senior Citizen/Disabled Tax Relief, or Assessment Cap Credit

application.file_with: District of Columbia Office of Veterans Affairs
application.timing_full_year: October 1 to March 31 for full tax year benefit
application.timing_half_year: April 1 to September 30 for half-year benefit on second-half tax bill
application.ongoing: Once approved, continues in future years if property continues to qualify

active_duty_members.documentation_required: DD-2058 (State of Legal Residence Certificate) showing DC as domicile

veterans_homestead_deduction.survivor_note: Standard veteran deduction does not automatically transfer to surviving spouse — separate surviving spouse provisions may apply

pending_legislation.bill: Disabled Veterans Complete Property Tax Exemption Amendment Act of 2025
pending_legislation.status: Referred to Committee on Business and Economic Development as of November 2025
pending_legislation.note: If enacted, would expand benefits beyond current deduction

veterans_homestead_deduction.authority: D.C. Law 22-283 (Disabled Veterans Homestead Exemption Amendment Act of 2018)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: 2025: $445,000 assessed value reduction for 100% P&T or IU veterans with household income <=159,750. For DC median home values (~$650K+), covers most or all taxable value.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $445,000 assessed value reduction for 100% P&T or IU veterans with household income ≤$159,750.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal pensions taxable but eligible for pension exclusion up to $12,500

military_retirement.note: Delaware allows $12,500 pension exclusion for military retired pay (no age requirement as of 2025). Pending legislation would increase to $25,000. This is part of the general pension/retirement income exclusion, not a military-specific statute.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits are exempt from Delaware income tax

sbp_annuities.sbp_note: SBP annuities are tax-free for survivors age 62+; otherwise taxable with pension exclusion available

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates up to 6.6% on income over $60,000 (2025)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Delaware veteran-specific benefits. No statewide full property tax exemption for disabled veterans, but county-administered exemptions exist (New Castle County offers full exemption for 100% P&T). School tax credit available statewide. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_school_tax_credit.description: 100% credit against non-vocational school district property tax on primary residence for veterans with 100% P&T or IU disability rating. County and municipal taxes are separate and not covered by this statewide credit.

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving 100% disability compensation due to individual unemployability qualify
eligibility.delaware_resident_note: Must have been legal Delaware resident for 3 years prior to application

application.where: County assessment office (New Castle, Kent, or Sussex)
application.form: Application for Disabled Veteran School Property Tax Credit
application.filing_deadline: April 30
application.annual_reapplication_note: Once approved, veterans do not need to reapply

county_property_tax_exemption.description: County-administered full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans. Coverage varies by county. New Castle County fully exempts qualifying veterans from county property taxes. Kent and Sussex counties may have different provisions.
county_property_tax_exemption.county_note: Each county sets additional eligibility requirements. New Castle County provides full exemption; Kent and Sussex may vary.

eligibility.domicile_period_note: Period varies by county (New Castle: 10 consecutive years; 12-month waiver for 100% disabled veterans)

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full tax credit on school district portion of property taxes for 100% P&T or IU veterans with at least 3 years Delaware residency. County and municipal portions still apply.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full tax credit on school district property taxes for 100% P&T or IU veterans with 3+ years DE residency.

income_tax.note: Florida has no state income tax. No tax on FERS pension, TSP withdrawals, SS, or any retirement income.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Standard homestead exemption of up to $50,000 for primary residence. First $25,000 applies to all property taxes; additional $25,000 applies to non-school taxes only.

exemptions.name: Save Our Homes Assessment Cap
exemptions.note: Annual assessment increases capped at 3% for homestead properties

exemptions.name: Senior Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Additional exemption for seniors age 65+ with income below threshold. Amount varies by county.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exemptions for Florida. All require documentation from VA. Florida requires homestead exemption as foundation for most veteran benefits. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

total_permanent_disability_exemption.description: Total exemption from ad valorem property taxes on homestead for 100% P&T disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_note: Florida statute specifies 'total and permanent' — verify IU eligibility with county property appraiser
eligibility.residency_note: Must be permanent Florida resident as of January 1 of the tax year for which exemption is claimed

total_permanent_disability_exemption.homestead_note: Must also have standard Florida Homestead Exemption on the property
total_permanent_disability_exemption.commercial_note: Any portion of property used for commercial purposes is not exempt
total_permanent_disability_exemption.late_application: Late applications accepted through approximately September 20 (25 days after TRIM notice) with explanation

required: VA letter certifying service-connected total and permanent disability

prorated_refund.note: Veterans acquiring new homestead property between January 1 and November 1 may receive prorated refund of taxes paid if they held veteran exemption on prior property

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: If surviving spouse sells and moves, exemption on new property capped at amount from most recent tax roll of prior property

partial_disability_exemption.description: $5,000 exemption in assessed value for veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability

eligibility.rating_note: Any service-connected disability rating of 10% or more qualifies

partial_disability_exemption.homestead_note: This exemption is NOT limited to homestead property — can apply to any property veteran owns

combat_disability_discount.description: Property tax discount commensurate with disability percentage for veterans age 65+ with combat-related disability

eligibility.rating_note: Discount percentage equals veteran's service-connected disability percentage

survivor_conditions.effective_note: Amendment 6 (passed November 2020) allows surviving spouses to retain discount

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption from property taxes on homestead for 100% P&T veterans (FL Stat. Sec. 196.081). Also applies to veterans confined to wheelchair. Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on homestead for 100% P&T disabled veterans. Surviving unremarried spouse retains.

military_retirement.exemption_note: Up to $65,000 of military retirement pay exempt for all ages (HB 266, signed May 13, 2025, effective tax year 2026). Prior age-based tiers ($17,500 under 62 / $35,000 ages 62-64 / $65,000 age 65+) replaced.

prior_tiers._note: Pre-2026 tiers retained for historical reference and prior-year filing

military_retirement.note: Georgia exempts up to $65,000 of military retirement pay from state income tax for all ages effective 2026 tax year (HB 266, signed May 13, 2025 by Gov. Kemp). Removes prior age-based tier structure. Does not cover USPHS or NOAA commissioned corps retirement.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Georgia does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.note: Georgia flat income tax rate of 5.09% (2026). Phase-down: 5.39% (pre-2025), 5.19% (2025), 5.09% (2026), further reductions to 4.99% pending revenue triggers.

exemptions.name: Standard Homestead Exemption

exemptions.name: Senior School Tax Exemption
exemptions.note: Seniors 62+ may be exempt from school taxes on homestead — varies by county

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Georgia. All require documentation from VA. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Exemption from homestead property taxes up to federally-indexed amount for qualifying disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans rated individually unemployable (IU) and compensated at 100% also qualify

Loss of sight in one or both eyes
Permanent impairment of vision in both eyes to a prescribed degree

pending_legislation.proposed_effective: January 1, 2027 if approved by voters November 2026

vehicle_ad_valorem_exemption.description: Exemption from Ad Valorem tax (annual property tax or title tax) on one vehicle owned by qualifying disabled veteran

Loss or permanent loss of use of one or both hands
Loss of sight in one or both eyes
Permanent impairment of vision in both eyes to a prescribed degree

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.exemption_amount_note: Amount as of current year — may be indexed

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Partial assessed value exemption (~$121,812 in 2025, CPI-indexed) for 100% disabled or IU veterans.

states._territory_note: Unincorporated US territory using a mirror tax code — residents file with the Guam Department of Revenue and Taxation at federal income tax rates, not with the IRS.

income_tax._mirror_note: Guam mirrors the federal IRC. Residents pay taxes at federal rates to Guam DRT, not the IRS. Federal tax brackets and standard deduction apply.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is taxable in Guam at federal rates

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and excluded from Guam taxable income

dic.note: Dependency and Indemnity Compensation is tax-free under both federal and Guam mirror code

sbp.note: Survivor Benefit Plan annuities are taxable in Guam at federal rates

disability_retirement_pay.note: Military disability retirement pay for service-connected injury/sickness is excluded from taxable income per IRS Publication 525

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits taxed under the same rules as federal — partially taxable based on combined income thresholds

income_tax.note: Guam mirrors federal tax rates. Top marginal rate is 37% (same as federal). Residents file with Guam DRT, not IRS.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Guam offers homestead exemptions for primary residences — contact Guam DRT for current limits

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Guam. All require documentation from VA. Federal benefits are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section. Guam offers 14 territory-level veteran benefits per CNAS analysis.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from all Guam property taxes on one residence for 100% disabled or individually unemployable veterans

survivor_conditions.minor_dependents_note: Minor dependent children supervised by a legal guardian qualify

Guam does not appear to levy an annual vehicle personal property tax in the manner of states like Virginia or Mississippi. Veterans with VA-provided vehicles may have separate license/registration benefits.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Disabled veteran property tax benefits are locally administered. Contact Guam Department of Revenue and Taxation.

military_retirement.note: Hawaii does not tax military retired pay or SBP payments

national_guard_drill_pay.note: Exempt up to E-5/8 years equivalent for 48 drills plus 15 days annual training

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Hawaii does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Hawaii graduated income tax, top bracket 11%

exemptions.name: Basic Homeowner Exemption

totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes (except minimum tax and special assessments) on primary residence for totally disabled veterans
totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.county_note: Each county has its own assessment rate, eligibility requirements, forms, and deadlines. Benefit structure is similar but details vary.

ownership_requirements.spouse_note: Property owned by veteran together with spouse qualifies

totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.commercial_note: If any portion used for commercial purposes, that portion is not exempt
totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.subletting_exception: May sublet not more than one room to a tenant (Honolulu)

county_deadlines.honolulu: On or before August 19 (first half) or February 19 (second half)
county_deadlines.hawaii_county: On or before June 30 (first half) or December 31 (second half)

documentation: Physician certification of disability or VA documentation

totally_disabled_veteran_exemption.long_term_care_note: Exemption continues if veteran moves to long-term care facility or adult residential care home (Honolulu)

vehicle_registration_fee_exemption.description: $46 state registration fee exemption on one vehicle for 100% disabled veterans

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: County-administered. Honolulu provides full exemption from property taxes (except minimum tax) for totally disabled veterans. Other counties have similar programs with varying details. Contact your county real property assessment division.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: County-administered; Honolulu provides full exemption for totally disabled veterans. Details vary by county.

military_retirement.note: Iowa fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: 100% P&T or IU veterans receive tax credit equal to entire property tax bill — effectively a full exemption.

Does not reduce solid waste, irrigation, or other fees. Surviving spouse can continue benefit but not transfer to new property after veteran's death.

standard_homestead_exemption_for_veteran.commercial_note: Portion of property used for commercial purposes is excluded

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Exemption on up to $100,000 of equalized assessed value for 100% disabled veterans. Effectively full for most homes.

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouses of eligible veterans may qualify for the deductions

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Deduction of $14,000 from assessed value for totally disabled veterans on primary residence (assessed value <=240,000). Separate wartime disability deduction of $24,960 also available for qualifying wartime veterans with 10%+ rating.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $14,000 assessed value deduction for totally disabled veterans on primary residence (assessed value ≤$240,000).

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 5.7% on income over $30,000 single / $60,000 joint (2025)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Kansas veteran-specific benefits. No full property tax exemption for disabled veterans — uses income-based homestead refund program. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_refund.description: Refund of a portion of property taxes paid on homestead. Amount based on household income; maximum refund $700. Not a full exemption. Filed on Form K-40SVR.

application.form: K-40SVR (or K-40H for standard homestead claim)
application.filing_deadline: April 15 of following year (filed with income tax return)

income_tax.note: Kentucky flat income tax rate of 3.5% (2026). Reduced from 4.0% (2025). Further reductions toward 0% possible if revenue triggers met.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.note: Reduces assessed value of primary residence by up to $49,100 for 2025-2026 tax years. Adjusted biennially for inflation.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Kentucky veteran-specific property tax benefits. eligibility.iu_eligible: Verify with county PVA — statute references total disability
eligibility.veteran_specific_note: This is a general disability/age homestead exemption, not veteran-specific

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Homestead exemption of $49,100 assessed value for 100% P&T disabled veterans (2025-2026, inflation-adjusted every 2 years). P&T veterans do not need to reapply annually.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $49,100 assessed value homestead exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans (2025–2026, inflation-adjusted).

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full exemption from parish ad valorem property taxes on primary residence for 100% service-connected disabled veterans.

pending_legislation.note: If enacted, would significantly expand benefits beyond current Clause 22 amounts

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Massachusetts Clause 22 provides tiered veteran property tax exemptions administered by cities/towns. 100% disabled veterans may qualify for larger exemptions depending on municipality. Contact your local board of assessors.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Tiered veteran property tax exemptions administered by cities/towns. 100% disabled veterans may qualify for larger exemptions.

pending_legislation.title: Granting property tax exemptions to disabled veterans
pending_legislation.status: Reported favorably by Revenue Committee; referred to Senate Ways and Means (12/22/2025). Active.
pending_legislation.impact: Tiered property tax exemptions: $2,500 (30-50%), $5,000 (50-70%), full exemption (70%+)

pension_exclusion.note: Pension exclusion of up to $36,200 for taxpayers age 65+. Subject to income limits.

under_55.note: Up to $12,500 subtraction for military retirees under age 55

age_55_plus.note: Up to $20,000 subtraction for military retirees age 55 and older

pending_legislation.bills: HB 761 (Keep Our Heroes Home Act: $25K for 2026, $40K thereafter, all ages), HB 857 ($20K for under-55)
pending_legislation.status: Both died in House Ways and Means. HB 857 had hearing 2/19/2026 but missed March 23 crossover deadline — effectively dead for 2026 session (sine die April 13, 2026). $1.4B state deficit primary obstacle. HB 761 also failed to advance.

military_retirement.note: Maryland allows a subtraction of up to $12,500 of military retirement pay (under age 55) or up to $20,000 (age 55+). Delaware-style age removal not yet enacted. HB 761 and HB 857 would increase amounts. USPHS and NOAA retirees not currently covered.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Maryland does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.local_income_tax_note: Maryland counties impose local income tax in addition to state tax — typically 2.25% to 3.2% depending on county

exemptions.name: Homeowners' Property Tax Credit
exemptions.note: Maryland provides a homeowners' property tax credit based on income. Income-based, not age-based.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Maryland. All require documentation from VA (Summary of Benefits Letter). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

real_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from real property taxes on dwelling house and surrounding yard (curtilage) for 100% P&T disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans rated 100% permanently unemployable (IU) also qualify
eligibility.residency_note: Veteran must be a Maryland resident

real_property_tax_exemption.scope: Dwelling house and surrounding yard including structures necessary for residential use (garage, utility shed)

required: VA rating decision showing 100% service-connected permanent and total disability
Maryland residency proof (driver's license or prior year MD tax return)
documentation.va_letter_requirements: Must show 100% service-connected P&T rating, VA decision date, and disability effective date

real_property_tax_exemption.refund_note: Refunds available for up to 3 years from when veteran became initially eligible, starting with tax year 2018-2019

survivor_conditions.dic_note: Surviving spouse receiving DIC may also qualify
survivor_conditions.annual_refiling: May be required — consult local assessor's office

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Real property tax exemption for surviving spouse of active-duty military personnel who died in the line of duty

eligibility.condition: Service member died in the line of duty while on active duty

partial_disability_credit.description: County-administered property tax credits for veterans with disability ratings below 100%

rating_threshold_75.note: 50% county property tax credit for 75%+ disability rating

rating_threshold_50.note: 25% county property tax credit for 50%-74% disability rating

partial_disability_credit.scope: County or municipal property tax only — separate from state exemption
partial_disability_credit.note: Not all counties may offer this credit — contact local assessor to verify

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption from State and county property taxes on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans (MD Tax-Property Sec. 7-208, effective July 1, 2007). Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full exemption from State and county property taxes on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans.

pending_legislation.title: Income Tax - Subtraction Modification for Military Retirement Income - Individuals Under Age 55
pending_legislation.status: Hearing held Feb 19, 2026 in House Ways and Means. MACo (Maryland Association of Counties) submitted opposing testimony citing local revenue impact. 25 co-sponsors (bipartisan). No committee vote. Critical: Maryland Crossover Date was March 23, 2026 — HB 857 did not cross over, effectively dead for 2026 session unless extraordinary action. Session ends April 13 (sine die). Primary obstacle: $1.4B structural deficit.
pending_legislation.impact: Would increase under-55 military retirement subtraction from $12,500 to $20,000. Effective July 1, 2026.

pending_legislation.title: Income Tax - Subtraction Modification for Military Retirement Income (Keep Our Heroes Home Act)
pending_legislation.status: In House Ways and Means. No committee vote reported. Companion to recurring Keep Our Heroes Home legislation (HB 554/2023, HB 952/2024).
pending_legislation.impact: Would increase military retirement subtraction to $25,000 for 2026, then $40,000 for 2027+, for all ages. More aggressive than HB 857.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civilian pensions are generally taxable in Maine with pension income deduction available

military_retirement.note: Maine fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax. Expanded to include Space Force, USPHS, and NOAA Corps effective 2026 tax year.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Maine exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax (conforming to federal thresholds)

sbp_annuities.note: SBP annuities are exempt from Maine state income tax

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates up to 7.15% on income over $58,050 single / $116,100 joint (2025, indexed)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Maine veteran-specific benefits. Property tax exemption for wartime veterans age 62+ or 100% disabled. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Wartime veterans age 62 or older, or with 100% disability, receive $6,000 exemption from property taxes on primary residence. Paraplegic veterans with VA specially adapted housing grant may receive $50,000 exemption.

eligibility.age_or_disability: Age 62+ OR 100% disability OR receiving 100% disability benefits
eligibility.rating_required_note: 100% disability required if under age 62; wartime veterans 62+ qualify regardless of disability status
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving 100% disability benefits qualify

application.filing_deadline: April 1 (check with municipality)

survivor_conditions.unremarried_spouse_note: Unremarried surviving spouse of qualifying veteran may receive exemption

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Paraplegic and totally disabled veterans may receive exemption up to ~$9,000 of assessed value. Some municipalities offer additional programs.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Exemption up to ~$9,000 of assessed value for paraplegic and totally disabled veterans.

military_retirement.note: Military retirement benefits paid to retirees of the U.S. Armed Forces are exempt from Michigan individual income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Michigan does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Flat rate of 4.25%

exemptions.name: Homestead Property Tax Credit
exemptions.note: Credit for property taxes on primary residence. Filed with annual income tax return. Amount varies by income and taxes paid.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Michigan. Full homestead exemption for 100% P&T, SAH, or IU veterans. Beginning 2025, exemption no longer requires annual reapplication. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veterans_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes on primary residence (homestead) for qualifying disabled veterans. Eliminates all property taxes except special assessments.

criteria: 100% permanently and totally disabled as a result of military service (VA determination)
Receiving or received pecuniary assistance for specially adapted housing (VA certificate)
Rated by VA as individually unemployable (IU)
eligibility.iu_note: IU is explicitly listed as qualifying criterion under MCL 211.7b

application.form: Form 5107 — Affidavit for Disabled Veterans Exemption
application.deadline: January 1 through December 31 of the year exemption is requested
application.reapplication: Not required beginning January 1, 2025 — exemption continues until rescinded or revoked

survivor_conditions.note: Un-remarried surviving spouse retains exemption, including on homestead property acquired after veteran's death

disabled_veterans_property_tax_exemption.authority: MCL 211.7b; MCL 211.7c (2025 amendment eliminating annual reapplication)

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans (MCL 211.7b). As of 2025 no reapplication required — continues automatically. Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans. No reapplication required (2025).

military_retirement.note: Minnesota fully exempts military retirement pay and Survivor Benefit Plan payments from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Minnesota taxes Social Security benefits following federal rules but provides a state subtraction that reduces or eliminates tax for many retirees. Subtraction amount based on provisional income thresholds that differ from federal calculations. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 9.85% on income over $193,240 single / $284,810 joint (2025)

exemptions.name: Homestead Market Value Exclusion (Standard)
exemptions.note: Standard homestead market value exclusion for all homeowners. Amount varies by property value.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Minnesota veteran-specific property tax benefits. Market value exclusion program reduces taxable market value of homestead. Two tiers based on disability rating. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_market_value_exclusion.description: Reduces market value of homestead for property tax purposes. Two exclusion levels based on disability status. Not a tax exemption or forgiveness — reduces taxable market value.

tiers.note: Market value exclusion of up to $150,000 for veterans with 70% or higher service-connected disability
Market value exclusion of up to $300,000 for veterans with total (100%) and permanent disability or individual unemployability

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans with permanent individual unemployability qualify for the $300,000 tier
eligibility.primary_caregiver_note: If veteran does not own a homestead, their primary family caregiver may be eligible

application.filing_deadline: December 31 for taxes payable the following year
application.reapplication: Surviving spouses must apply to continue; veterans need not reapply

veteran_military_service_credit.description: Income tax credit of up to $750 for past military service
veteran_military_service_credit.note: Additional combat zone service credit available for post-2014 service

hf194_hf684_hf1297.status: Advanced by House Veterans and Military Affairs Division (2025-2026 session)
hf194_hf684_hf1297.would_do: Increase market value exclusion from $150,000 to $165,000-$200,000 for 70%+ disabled veterans and from $300,000 to $330,000-$400,000 for 100% P&T. Amounts unchanged since 2008.
hf194_hf684_hf1297.note: Multiple bills addressing same issue; exact amounts vary by bill version

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Disabled Veteran Market Value Exclusion (Minn. Stat. Sec. 273.13): $300,000 market value exclusion for 100% P&T or IU veterans. Effectively full for most Greater MN homes; partial for high-value Twin Cities homes. Primary caregiver may qualify if veteran does not own homestead.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $300,000 market value exclusion for 100% P&T or IU veterans. Effectively full for most Greater Minnesota homes.

pending_legislation.title: Exclusion amount increased for homesteads of veterans with a disability
pending_legislation.status: In House Taxes Committee. 12 authors as of March 12, 2026 (bipartisan, Rasmusson added 2/26, Gottfried added 3/12). No hearing scheduled. HF1297 (higher exclusion amounts) is a competing bill in the same committee. 2026 session started Feb 17; no committee votes on either bill through March 2026.
pending_legislation.impact: Would increase disabled veteran property tax homestead exclusion amount

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from Missouri income tax. Deducted on Form MO-A, Part 1, Line 10.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Missouri exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax

income_tax.sbp_note: SBP/RCSBP/RSFPP survivors may deduct up to $47,633 for 2025 tax year
income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 4.8% for 2025

exemptions.name: Property Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker)
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+, or age 60+ receiving surviving spouse benefits, or 100% disabled, or 100% disabled veteran
exemptions.income_limit_note: Income limits apply — see MO DOR for current thresholds
exemptions.note: Tax credit (not exemption) — reduces amount owed, does not eliminate property tax

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific benefits for Missouri. No general full property tax exemption for disabled veterans (only for former POWs). Property tax credit program provides limited relief. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_credit.description: Property tax credit (not exemption) for 100% disabled veterans. Maximum credit of $1,100 for homeowners or $750 for renters. Income limits apply.

eligibility.rating_note: 100% disabled as a result of military service (VA determination)
eligibility.documentation: VA letter indicating 100% disability from military service must be attached to Property Tax Credit form

disabled_veteran_property_tax_credit.note: Missouri does NOT offer a general full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans who are not former POWs. Legislation has been proposed (HB 921/HB 552) but not enacted.

former_pow_full_exemption.description: Complete property tax exemption on homestead for Missouri residents who are former POWs with 100% total service-connected disability

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Missouri Property Tax Credit: Maximum $1,100 credit for 100% disabled veteran homeowners (RSMo Sec. 135.010). Income limits apply. Note: Former POWs with 100% total service-connected disability qualify for full homestead exemption (RSMo Sec. 137.106).
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Maximum $1,100 property tax credit for 100% disabled veteran homeowners. Income limits apply.

states._territory_note: Commonwealth in political union with the US. CNMI mirrors the federal IRC as its income tax law, substituting 'CNMI' for 'United States.' Bona fide residents file with the CNMI Division of Revenue and Taxation, not the IRS.

income_tax._mirror_note: CNMI mirrors the federal IRC. Bona fide residents file with CNMI Division of Revenue and Taxation. Additional local wage/salary and earnings taxes may apply.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is taxable in the CNMI at federal rates

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and excluded from CNMI taxable income via mirror code

dic.note: DIC is tax-free under mirror code

disability_retirement_pay.note: Military disability retirement pay for service-connected injury/sickness is excluded from taxable income

ss_income.note: Social Security taxed under federal rules via mirror code

income_tax.note: CNMI mirrors federal tax rates with additional local taxes. Top marginal rate is 37% (federal mirror). Bona fide residents file with CNMI DRT.

property_tax.note: No specific general property tax exemptions identified for CNMI

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific benefits for the Northern Mariana Islands. CNMI offers 4 territory-level veteran benefits per CNAS analysis, primarily tax exemptions inherited from the mirror code and employment/education benefits.

veteran_benefits._no_property_exemption_note: No CNMI-specific disabled veteran property tax exemption has been identified. Veterans should contact the CNMI Division of Revenue and Taxation for current property tax relief programs.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Northern Mariana Islands: No veteran-specific property tax exemption identified. Contact CNMI Division of Revenue and Taxation.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: No veteran-specific property tax exemption identified for Northern Mariana Islands.

military_retirement.note: Mississippi fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Mississippi does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Mississippi exempts all retirement income from state income tax, including federal civilian pensions, military retirement, 401(k), IRA, 403(b), and annuity distributions. Early distributions may be taxable.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption (Standard)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Mississippi veteran-specific benefits. Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

vehicle_ad_valorem_exemption.description: Full exemption from motor vehicle ad valorem and privilege taxes for disabled veterans with 100% permanent service-connected disability or 70%+ non-permanent rating. Two vehicles per veteran.

eligibility.rating_note: 100% permanent service-connected disability, or at least 70% non-permanent service-connected disability
eligibility.non_permanent_renewal: Veterans with non-permanent rating must provide updated VA Benefit Summary Letter annually

license_plate.type: Disabled American Veteran plate with branch of service seal

survivor_conditions.spouse_note: Surviving unremarried spouse can retain or apply for plates under same terms

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal pensions generally taxable. Retirees 65+ may deduct up to $5,500 (2025).

military_retirement.exempt_note: Partial exemption for working Montana residents
military_retirement.note: Starting 2024, working Montana residents may deduct up to 50% of military retirement income or Montana-source wage income (whichever is less) for up to 5 consecutive years. Expires tax year 2033. Separately, taxpayers age 65+ receive a $5,500 subtraction from federal taxable income. No full military retirement exemption.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Montana taxes Social Security benefits. Taxpayers age 65+ receive a $5,500 subtraction from federal taxable income. Several income deductions including partial interest income deduction for 65+ were repealed in recent years. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 5.9% on income over $21,100 single / $42,200 joint (2025)
income_tax.note: Montana top marginal income tax rate of 5.65% (2026, HB337). Reduced from 5.9% (2025). Will drop to 5.4% for 2027.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Montana veteran-specific benefits. Income-based property tax rate reduction for 100% disabled veterans (MDV program). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.description: Montana Disabled Veteran Assistance Program (MDV) reduces the property tax rate on primary residence. Rate reduction is 50%, 70%, 80%, or 100% of normal tax rate depending on income and marital status. For homes on agricultural/forest land, benefit applies to home and one-acre homesite.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.reduction_tiers_note: Reduction percentage depends on income brackets updated annually by PCE inflation factor. For TY 2026: income thresholds approximately $61,000 (single) / $70,000 (married) / $53,000 (unmarried surviving spouse) for 100% reduction, with lower reductions at higher incomes.

eligibility.pt_note: Must be rated or paid at 100% disabled by VA for service-connected disability
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans paid at 100% rate due to individual unemployability qualify
eligibility.occupancy_requirement: Must live in home at least 7 months per year

application.where: Montana Department of Revenue field office or online
documentation: VA Benefit Summary Letter showing 100% service-connected disability
Federal tax return (if new MT resident)
application.filing_deadline: April 15 (late applications considered for following year)

disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.auto_renewal_note: Once approved, renews automatically as long as veteran continues to own, occupy, and meet income requirements

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Montana Disabled Veterans (MDV) program: 50-100% reduction in property tax rate on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans based on income (must not exceed ~$52,652 for 2025, inflation-indexed).
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: 50–100% reduction in property tax rate for 100% disabled veterans based on income (income limit ~$52,652 in 2025).

military_retirement.eligibility: Retired members who served at least 20 years OR medically retired under 10 U.S.C. Chapter 61
military_retirement.sbp_note: Survivor Benefit Plan payments to eligible beneficiaries are also deductible
military_retirement.tsp_note: TSP distributions are NOT exempt and are subject to state income tax
military_retirement.note: North Carolina fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax (Session Law 2021-180, effective January 1, 2021). Applies to Armed Forces retirees with 20+ years of service or medical retirees. SBP payments to eligible beneficiaries also deductible. va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits taxed on federal return may be deducted from NC state return

exemptions.name: Elderly or Disabled Exclusion
exemptions.note: Exclusion of up to $25,000 for homeowners 65+ or totally disabled with income limits

exemptions.name: Circuit Breaker Tax Deferment
exemptions.note: Tax deferment program for qualifying elderly/disabled homeowners

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exclusions for North Carolina. All require certification from NC Department of Military and Veterans Affairs. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exclusion.exclusion_amount_note: Current exclusion is first $45,000 of appraised value; pending legislation may increase this significantly

eligibility.iu_note: NC statute specifies 'permanent and total' — verify IU eligibility with county tax office
eligibility.specially_adapted_note: Veterans receiving VA Specially Adapted Housing benefits under 38 U.S.C. § 2101 also qualify

co_owner_rules.non_spouse_co_owners: Each eligible co-owner gets $45,000, up to $90,000 total

application.form: NCDVA-9 (Certification of Disabled Veteran's Property Tax Exclusion)
application.certification_by: NC Department of Military and Veterans Affairs or County Veterans Service Office

survivor_conditions.service_connected_death_note: Surviving spouse of veteran who died from service-connected condition also qualifies

pending_legislation.bill: S 660 / H 39 (Honoring Sacrifice: NC Veterans Relief Act)
pending_legislation.status: S 660 referred to Senate Rules (03/26/2025). H 39 referred to House Finance (02/05/2025). H 728 referred to House Rules (04/03/2025). None advanced in 2025 session; all carryover for 2026.

proposed_changes.amount: Lesser of $500,000 or 100% of appraised value

pending_legislation.vehicle_note: Would also exclude primary motor vehicle owned by 100% disabled veteran from property tax

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

Disabled veterans with VA-adapted vehicles are exempt from the highway-use tax. The pending Veterans Relief Act (S 660 / H 39) would also exclude the primary motor vehicle of 100% disabled veterans from property tax. disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Disabled Veterans' Homestead Property Tax Exclusion (G.S. 105-277.1C): $45,000 of assessed value excluded from taxation for 100% P&T disabled veterans. Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $45,000 of assessed value excluded from taxation for 100% P&T disabled veterans.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civilian pensions generally taxable. North Dakota allows a partial pension deduction depending on specific criteria.

military_retirement.note: Military retirement pay fully exempt from North Dakota income tax (effective 2019). Active duty military pay also exempt (SB 2293, effective 2023).

application.form: Application for Disabled Veterans Property Tax Credit

documentation: VA certification of service-connected disability percentage

survivor_conditions.dic_spouse_note: Surviving spouse receiving VA Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) receives 100% credit regardless of veteran's rating at death

disabled_veteran_property_tax_credit.married_veterans_note: If two disabled veterans are married and living together, combined credits may not exceed 100% of $9,000 taxable value

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: 100% disabled veterans may qualify for property tax credit on up to $120,000 of assessed value. Scales with disability rating (50%+ required). Contact county auditor.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Property tax credit on up to $120,000 of assessed value for 100% disabled veterans (50%+ rating required).

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

sbp_annuities.sbp_note: SBP, RCSBP, and RSFPP annuities are not taxed in Nebraska
