income_tax.note: Nebraska top marginal income tax rate of 4.55% (2026). Phase-down from 5.2% (2025). Will drop to 3.99% for 2027+.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Nebraska veteran-specific benefits. Full homestead exemption for 100% service-connected permanently disabled veterans (Category 4V). IU eligible. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Category 4V: Veterans with 100% permanent service-connected disability or 100% IU rating receive full property tax exemption on homestead regardless of income or homestead value. Category 7 covers 100% temporary service-connected disability (also full exemption). Non-service-connected totally disabled veterans receive partial exemption based on income (Category 3).

eligibility.pt_note: Category 4V requires 100% permanent service-connected disability. Category 7 covers 100% temporary service-connected disability.
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans with 100% Individual Unemployability (IU) rating qualify for Category 4V
eligibility.discharge_note: Honorable or general (under honorable conditions) discharge required
eligibility.occupancy_requirement: Must own and occupy homestead from January 1 through August 15 of the application year

application.filing_period: After February 1 and on or before June 30
application.renewal_note: Category 4V: Required at initial filing and in years ending in 0 or 5. Category 7: Required annually.

survivor_conditions.unremarried_spouse_note: Surviving spouse may remarry after age 57 and retain eligibility
survivor_conditions.kia_spouse_note: Unremarried surviving spouse of service member who died from service-connected disability

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.pending_legislation_note: LB 272 (2025) would extend partial homestead exemption to veterans with 10-99% disability proportional to rating. LB 425 would set threshold at 80%. Both carryover for 2026 short session (Jan 7 - Apr 17, 2026). Neither designated 2026 priority; session focused on $471M budget deficit. LB 650 (2025) vehicle tax exemption was enacted — see vehicle_tax_exemption entry. Last checked: 2026-03-23.

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Motor vehicle tax and motor vehicle fee exemption for one vehicle owned and used for personal transportation by a disabled or blind veteran. Covers any service-connected disability rating (including 0%). Does NOT include plate fee, sales tax, wheel tax, or other fees.

eligibility.rating_note: Any service-connected disability rating (including 0%) per 5 U.S.C. § 2108 definition. Also covers veterans receiving VA compensation, disability retirement benefits, or pension, and blind veterans.
eligibility.iu_note: IU-rated veterans qualify as they receive VA compensation
eligibility.vehicle_limit_note: ONE vehicle owned and used for personal transportation. If veteran has multiple vehicles, they choose which one to apply the exemption to.

application.where: Nebraska Department of Veterans' Affairs Military & Veterans' Registry
documentation: VA benefit summary letter showing service-connected disability rating or VA compensation
Must appear on NDVA Military & Veterans' Registry
application.timing_note: Must apply within 30 days of registration renewal date or new vehicle purchase date. Registry approval may take up to 48 hours to sync with DMV.

vehicle_tax_exemption._scope_note: Exemption covers Motor Vehicle Tax and Motor Vehicle Fee only. Plate fee, sales tax, wheel tax, and other fees still apply.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Nebraska Homestead Exemption Category 4V: Full exemption for veterans with 100% service-connected disability — no income limit or homestead value limit. Surviving unremarried spouse may continue to qualify.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption (Nebraska Homestead Category 4V) for 100% service-connected disabled veterans. No income or value limit.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: New Hampshire has no state income tax (interest/dividend tax repealed effective 2025)

military_retirement.note: No state income tax; military retirement is not taxed

va_compensation.note: No state income tax

ss_income.note: No state income tax

income_tax.rate_note: New Hampshire has no state income tax. Interest and dividend tax was repealed effective January 1, 2025.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: New Hampshire veteran-specific benefits. Full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans. Municipal-option credits for wartime and disabled veterans. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Veterans with 100% permanent and total service-connected disability receive full property tax exemption on primary residence. Qualifying surviving spouses may continue.

eligibility.iu_note: Statute references 100% permanently and totally disabled; IU eligibility not explicitly addressed in statute

application.filing_deadline: Contact local assessor for municipal deadlines

wartime_veteran_property_tax_credit.description: Municipal-option property tax credit for wartime veterans. Standard credit $50-$500 (varies by municipality); towns may adopt higher amounts. Disabled veterans may receive $700-$4,000 credit depending on municipality.
wartime_veteran_property_tax_credit.note: Amounts vary significantly by municipality. Contact local assessor to verify adopted amounts.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: RSA 72:36-a: 100% P&T veterans may qualify for full property tax exemption in municipalities that have adopted the program. Contact your local assessor — benefits vary by town.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: 100% P&T veterans may qualify for full exemption in municipalities that have adopted RSA 72:36-a. Benefits vary by town.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civil service pensions (OPM) are taxable even if based on military service credit

military_retirement.note: U.S. military pension and survivor's benefit payments are not taxable in New Jersey

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation and total/permanent disability income conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits are not taxable in New Jersey

sbp_annuities.note: SBP, RCSBP, and RSFPP annuities are exempt from New Jersey income tax

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates up to 10.75% on income over $1M (2025). Pension exclusion: up to $100,000 joint/$75,000 single for qualifying retirement income.

veteran_income_tax_exemption.note: Honorably discharged veterans receive a $6,000 personal exemption on NJ income tax; joint filers with veteran spouse may also claim

exemptions.name: Veteran's Property Tax Deduction
exemptions.eligibility: Honorably discharged wartime veteran or unremarried surviving spouse/civil union partner of veteran or service member killed on active duty
exemptions.note: $250 annual deduction from property taxes. Filed with municipal tax assessor or collector using Form V.S.S.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: New Jersey veteran-specific benefits. Full property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans. Proportional exemption legislation pending. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Complete property tax exemption on primary residence and lot for 100% permanently and totally disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans deemed unemployable by VA and compensated at 100% rate qualify for full exemption

application.filing_deadline: File with local assessor; appeals to County Board of Taxation by April 1

s522_proportional_exemption.would_do: Proportional property tax exemption for veterans with 30-90% disability rating, capped at $10,000. IU veterans would receive full exemption. Surviving spouses limited to those of 100% exempt veterans.
s522_proportional_exemption.note: Estimated $228-376M state cost in first year

a941_retroactive_exemption.would_do: Retroactive property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans to date of VA disability determination. Municipalities would refund taxes collected after determination date.

s204_expanded_survivor.would_do: Expand disabled veteran property tax exemption eligibility to include certain individuals with close personal relationship to deceased veteran

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans (N.J.S.A. 54:4-3.30). Surviving unremarried spouse also qualifies. Separate $250 annual veteran deduction also available.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans (N.J.S.A. 54:4-3.30).

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civilian pensions are taxable in New Mexico

military_retirement.note: New Mexico exempts up to $40,000 of military retired pay (2025+, increased from $30,000 in 2024). All income exempt for taxpayers age 100+. Low-income taxpayers 65+ may also qualify for separate $8,000 exclusion (phases out above $28,500 single / $51,000 joint).

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: New Mexico taxes Social Security for higher-income filers. Full exemption for single filers with AGI up to $100,000 and joint filers up to $150,000. Above those thresholds, benefits taxed under standard state rates. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

sbp_annuities.sbp_note: SBP annuities are taxable beyond the $40,000 military retirement exclusion

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates up to 5.9% on income over $210,000 single / $315,000 joint (2025)

exemptions.name: Standard Veteran Property Tax Exemption
exemptions.note: $10,000 standard veteran property tax exemption for all honorably discharged veterans with 90+ days active duty service (effective 2025, CPI-indexed)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: New Mexico veteran-specific benefits. Proportional disabled veteran property tax exemption by disability percentage (2026+). 100% P&T full exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: 100% permanently and totally disabled veterans receive full property tax exemption on primary residence. Starting 2026, proportional exemption available based on VA disability percentage (e.g., 50% rating = 50% property tax reduction). Unremarried surviving spouse of eligible veteran or service member who died in line of duty also eligible.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.proportional_note: New amendment (ratified Nov 2024) authorizes proportional reduction by VA disability percentage starting 2026

eligibility.rating_required_note: 100% P&T for full exemption; any rating for proportional (2026+)
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving 100% compensation due to individual unemployability qualify for full exemption

application.filing_deadline: Contact county assessor

survivor_conditions.killed_in_line_of_duty_note: 2024 amendment expanded to include spouses of service members who died in line of duty (not just KIA)

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.authority: NM Constitution Article VIII; NM Taxation and Revenue Dept; HB 47 (2024)

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans and surviving unremarried spouses (NMSA Sec. 7-37-5.1).
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans and surviving spouses.

income_tax.note: Nevada has no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Surviving Spouse Exemption
exemptions.note: CPI-indexed from $1,000 base

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and vehicle tax benefits for Nevada. No state income tax. Exemptions are assessed value deductions (assessed value ≈ 35% of taxable value). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

wartime_veteran_exemption.description: Assessed value deduction for wartime veterans, applicable to real property, personal property, or vehicle registration
wartime_veteran_exemption.amount_note: CPI-indexed annually from $2,000 base (2003). Assessed value ≈ 35% of taxable value.

eligibility.service_requirement: 90+ consecutive days active duty during qualifying wartime period
eligibility.qualifying_periods_note: Includes WWII, Korea, Vietnam, Persian Gulf, and other designated periods per NRS 361.090
eligibility.residency_note: Must be bona fide NV resident (6+ months or valid NV driver's license)

application_to.split_note: Can split exemption between multiple categories or use all within one
application_to.donate_note: May donate exemption to Gift Account for Veterans' Homes

wartime_veteran_exemption.cannot_combine_note: Recipients of disabled veteran exemption (NRS 361.091) cannot also claim this wartime exemption

disabled_veteran_exemption.description: Tiered assessed value deduction based on disability percentage for veterans with permanent service-connected disability of 60% or more
disabled_veteran_exemption.tier_note: Amounts CPI-indexed annually. Approximate savings vary by county tax rate.

eligibility.rating_note: Permanent service-connected disability of 60% or more. Combined percentages allowed (not to exceed 100%).
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Statute references 'permanent service-connected disability' with specific percentage — IU not explicitly addressed; recommend verification with county assessor

application.deadline: On or before June 15 for following fiscal year (real property)
documentation: VA certificate or military document showing permanent service-connected disability and percentage

survivor_conditions.marriage_requirement: Married to and living with eligible disabled veteran for 5 years prior to death
survivor_conditions.combined_exemptions_note: Surviving spouse of disabled veteran who is also a veteran can receive BOTH this surviving spouse exemption AND their own wartime veteran exemption

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: NRS 361.091: At 100% disability: $35,400 assessed value deduction (FY2025/2026, CPI-indexed). Requires 60%+ permanent service-connected disability (at minimum). Applicable to real property, manufactured home, or vehicle registration. Low benefit relative to NV home values.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $35,400 assessed value deduction at 100% disability rating (FY2025/2026, CPI-indexed) for 60%+ service-connected disabled veterans.

income_tax.note: New York exempts all government pensions (federal, state, local, military) from state income tax under the State Constitution.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from NY State, NYC, and Yonkers income taxes

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: New York does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates from 4% to 10.9% (plus NYC local tax up to 3.876% for NYC residents)

exemptions.name: STAR Exemption
exemptions.note: School Tax Relief — reduces school property taxes for owner-occupied primary residences. Basic STAR and Enhanced STAR (age 65+) available.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for New York. Three exemption types available — local option. Amounts set by municipality. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

alternative_veterans_exemption.description: Local-option exemption for wartime veterans. Three tiers: basic wartime service, combat zone service, and disability. Amounts set by each county/city/town/village/school district.
alternative_veterans_exemption.local_option_note: Each taxing jurisdiction independently decides whether to offer this exemption and at what level

wartime.max_exemption_note: Up to 15% of assessed value, capped at locality-set maximum

combat_zone.additional_note: Additional 10% of assessed value for combat zone service, subject to local cap

disability.max_note: Up to $40,000 or the product of $40,000 × latest equalization rate, whichever is less

cold_war_veterans_exemption.description: Local-option exemption for veterans who served during the Cold War period. Similar structure to alternative veterans exemption.

s1183_a74.status: Passed Senate; in Assembly committee (2025-2026 session)
s1183_a74.would_do: Full property tax exemption for 100% service-connected disabled veterans (including IU) on primary residence. Local option.
s1183_a74.note: Has passed the NY Senate in multiple sessions but has not yet been signed into law

_survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouses and Gold Star parents may qualify

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: New York Alternative Veterans Exemption (RPTL Sec. 458-a): 100% service-connected disabled veterans may qualify for larger exemptions on assessed value. Amounts vary by taxing jurisdiction. Contact your local assessor.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Local-option exemption; amounts vary by taxing jurisdiction. 100% disabled veterans may qualify for larger assessed value reductions.

military_retirement.note: Ohio fully exempts all military retirement pay from state income tax. No income limits or age restrictions.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Ohio does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 3.57% for income over $115,300 (2025)
income_tax.note: Ohio flat income tax rate of 2.75% on income over $26,050 (2026, HB96). Transitioned from two-rate system (2.75%/3.125% in 2025) to flat rate.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption (Standard)
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+ or permanently and totally disabled
exemptions.note: Standard homestead exemption shields first $29,000 (2026) of appraised value from taxation

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Ohio. Enhanced homestead exemption for 100% disabled veterans with no income limit. SB 92 (pending) would provide full exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Enhanced homestead exemption for 100% disabled veterans. Reduces appraised value of primary residence. No income limit for disabled veterans (unlike standard homestead).

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving compensation at 100% rate due to individual unemployability qualify

application.where: County auditor in county where home is located
application.filing: Initial application required; no subsequent reapplication needed
documentation: VA award letter showing 100% or IU

sb_92.would_do: Total property tax exemption for totally disabled veterans and surviving spouses on primary residence. Effective tax year 2025 for real property, 2026 for manufactured homes.
sb_92.note: Would replace partial enhanced exemption with full exemption

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans (ORC Sec. 323.152). Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T disabled veterans.

military_retirement.note: 100% of income received as salary or compensation by an Oklahoma resident as a member of any component of the Armed Forces is deductible from taxable income, including retired pay

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Oklahoma does not tax Social Security benefits included in federal AGI

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 4.75% on income over $7,200 single / $12,200 joint (2025)
income_tax.note: Oklahoma top marginal income tax rate of 4.5% (2026, HB2764). Brackets consolidated from 6 to 3. Rate reduction triggers toward 0% enacted.

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: Assessed value, not market value. Results in approximately $80-$120 tax savings depending on millage rate.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Oklahoma veteran-specific benefits. Full property tax exemption for 100% disabled veterans (constitutional provision). Sales tax and motor vehicle exemptions also available. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from property tax on the fair cash value of homestead property for 100% permanently disabled veterans and surviving spouses. Constitutional provision effective January 1, 2006.

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans in receipt of compensation at the 100% rate qualify
eligibility.homestead_note: Must have homestead exemption in place or be eligible. Must own and occupy as of January 1.

documentation: VA property tax letter with raised seal certifying 100% permanent disability
application.filing_deadline: Deed recorded by February 1; file anytime
application.reapplication: Not required unless qualifications change or property changes

disabled_veteran_sales_tax_exemption.description: 100% permanently disabled veterans exempt from sales tax on up to $25,000 in annual purchases (excludes tobacco, alcohol, vehicles). Surviving spouses: up to $1,000.

disabled_veteran_motor_vehicle_exemption.description: Motor vehicle excise tax exemption for 100% permanently disabled veterans. One vehicle per consecutive three-year period. Surviving spouses not eligible.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption from ad valorem property taxes on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans (68 O.S. Sec. 2889). Surviving unremarried spouse may continue to qualify.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full exemption from ad valorem property taxes on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans.

military_retirement.note: Military retirement pay is taxable in Oregon for service after October 1, 1991. Service before that date may qualify for a deduction. Oregon has no special military retirement exemption.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Oregon does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Oregon graduated income tax, top bracket 9.9%

exemptions.name: Senior/Disabled Property Tax Deferral
exemptions.note: Oregon has no general homestead exemption. Seniors may defer property taxes under specific programs.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Oregon. Benefits are assessed value deductions that increase 3% annually. Oregon has no general homestead exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.description: Assessed value deduction on primary residence for disabled veterans or surviving spouses. Two tiers based on service-connection.
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.annual_increase_note: Both amounts increase by 3% each year
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.additional_pension_note: Additional $2,000 if veteran currently receives a pension and lives on the homestead property

eligibility.rating_note: 40% or more disability certified by VA, branch of service, or licensed physician
eligibility.service_connected_note: Service-connected disability qualifies for higher exemption amount ($31,565 vs $26,303)
eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Requires disability certification at 40%+ — IU with 40%+ combined rating should qualify; recommend verification
eligibility.wartime_service_note: 91+ consecutive days beginning on or before Jan 31, 1955, or 178+ days beginning after Jan 31, 1955

ownership_requirements.temporary_absence_note: Temporary absences due to vacation, travel, or illness do not disqualify

application.file_with: County Assessor where property is located
application.deadline: January 1 through April 1 for following tax year (beginning July 1)
application.late_acquisition_note: Properties acquired between March 1 and July 1: must file within 30 days of acquisition
application.renewal: Not required annually for permanent disabilities; must refile by April 1 if ownership or use changes
documentation: VA disability certification or physician certification

disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.prior_year_note: If VA delay caused late certification, veteran may qualify for prior year exemptions if disability existed at time of VA examination
disabled_veteran_homestead_exemption.exemption_applied_first_to: Home, then to taxable personal property

survivor_conditions.domestic_partner_note: Registered domestic partner qualifies same as surviving spouse
survivor_conditions.qualification_note: Surviving spouse of veteran who died of service-connected cause, OR veteran who received at least one year of maximum exemption

transition_to_credits.note: Oregon legislation proposes converting property tax exemptions to property tax credits starting 2026-27 tax year. Credit amounts: ~$475 basic, ~$575 service-connected. New participants in exemption programs may be disallowed after 2025-26.

active_duty_military_homestead_exemption.description: Assessed value deduction on primary residence for Oregon National Guard and Reserve members called to federal active duty

eligibility.requirement: Oregon resident National Guard or Reserve member called to federal active duty — at least one day of qualified service during tax year

application.deadline: On or before August 1 following end of tax year claimed

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption from property taxes on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans (ORS Sec. 307.250). Surviving unremarried spouse may qualify.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans.

income_tax.note: Pennsylvania does not tax qualified retirement benefits including military retirement, FERS/CSRS, and Social Security.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is fully exempt from PA personal income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Pennsylvania does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: PA flat tax of 3.07% — among the lowest income tax rates in the nation

exemptions.name: Property Tax/Rent Rebate Program
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+, widows/widowers age 50+, or permanently disabled age 18+
exemptions.note: Rebates on property taxes and rent for qualifying low-income Pennsylvanians

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for Pennsylvania. Requires wartime service and financial need determination. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_real_estate_tax_exempti.description: Full exemption from real property taxes on primary residence for qualifying 100% disabled wartime veterans who demonstrate financial need

eligibility.iu_note: Total disability individual unemployability (IU) explicitly qualifies under 51 Pa.C.S. Ch. 89
specific_disabilities: Loss of two or more limbs due to military service
eligibility.wartime_service_note: Must have served during a period of war as determined by VA, or received Armed Forces Expeditionary Medal

financial_need_test.threshold_adjustment: Biennial CPI adjustment (Northeast All Items CPI-U index)
financial_need_test.above_threshold_note: Applicants above threshold may still qualify if allowable monthly expenses exceed monthly household income. Real estate tax liability counted as an expense.
financial_need_test.va_benefits_note: VA financial benefits are not included in gross annual income calculation

ownership_requirements.note: Dwelling must be owned solely by veteran or as estate by the entirety with spouse

application.deadline_note: Must file before tax period commences for that period's exemption
documentation: MA-VA Form 40 (veteran) or MA-VA Form 40ss (surviving spouse)
Income/expense documentation if above presumptive threshold
application.processing_time: 120 days to complete documentation after initial filing

survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouse of qualifying veteran may continue exemption if financial need is demonstrated

pending_legislation.bill_1_proposed: Would remove wartime service requirement and add graduated reductions for 10%+ disability ($7,500-$15,000 based on rating)
pending_legislation.bill_2_proposed: Constitutional amendment to expand to all 100% disabled veterans regardless of wartime service, plus proportional exemption for 50%+ disability

disabled_veteran_real_estate_tax_exempti.authority: 51 Pa.C.S. Ch. 89; 43 Pa. Code Ch. 5 Sub-Chapter C; PA Constitution Art. VIII, § 2(c)

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: See federal_veteran_benefits for VA funding fee exemption details

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Disabled Veterans' Real Estate Tax Exemption Act (Act 77 of 2021): Full exemption for 100% P&T veterans demonstrating financial need. Presumptive need level $114,637 (2025 — verify current at PA DMVA). Surviving spouse may also qualify.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full exemption for 100% P&T veterans demonstrating financial need; presumptive income threshold $114,637 (2025).

states._territory_note: Commonwealth of the US with its own tax code (not a mirror code). Residents file with the Puerto Rico Departamento de Hacienda, not the IRS. PR-sourced income is exempt from federal income tax for bona fide residents. Social Security and Medicare taxes apply.

income_tax._code_note: Puerto Rico has its own Internal Revenue Code separate from the federal IRC. Bona fide residents do not pay federal income tax on PR-sourced income but file with Hacienda.

military_retirement.note: Legislation signed August 2024 exempts all military retired pay from territorial taxes, effective for 2025 tax year. Implementation by Hacienda and the Office of the Veterans Advocate is ongoing.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not subject to PR territorial taxes

sbp.note: Survivor Benefit Plan annuities are taxable in Puerto Rico

veteran_personal_exemption.note: Veterans may claim an additional $1,500 personal tax exemption ($3,000 if both spouses are veterans filing jointly). Available for life.

federal_pension_deduction.note: Retirees receiving a US government pension or annuity may deduct $11,000 (under 60) or $15,000 (60+) from territorial taxable income. Applies to FERS annuity, CSRS, and other federal pensions.

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits are not subject to Puerto Rico income tax

income_tax.note: Puerto Rico has its own progressive tax system. Top rate is 33%. First $9,000 is tax-free. Federal income tax does NOT apply to PR-sourced income for bona fide residents. Social Security and Medicare payroll taxes apply.

exemptions.name: Standard Veteran Property Tax Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: Up to $5,000 of appraised taxable value of primary residence
exemptions.eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and unremarried surviving spouses
exemptions.land_limit: 1,000 square meters in urban zone or 1 cuerda (3,930 sq meters) in rural zone
exemptions.note: Standard exemption for veteran-owned primary residence. Administered by CRIM (Centro de Recaudacion de Ingresos Municipales).

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property, vehicle, and housing benefits for Puerto Rico. All require documentation from VA. Puerto Rico offers 13 territory-level veteran benefits per CNAS analysis. Land measurements use 'cuerda' (1 cuerda ≈ 3,930 sq meters ≈ 0.971 acres).

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Tiered real estate property tax exemption based on disability rating, from partial to full exemption

tiers.name: 50%+ Service-Connected Disability
tiers.exemption_note: Exemption on first $50,000 of appraised taxable value of primary residence
tiers.documentation: Written VA certification of disability rating as of January 1 of the tax year
tiers.residency_note: Veteran or veteran's family must have resided at dwelling from January 1 of year immediately preceding the year of requested exemption

tiers.name: 100% Disabled Veteran Full Exemption
tiers.exemption_note: Complete exemption from property taxes on primary residence
tiers.family_note: Applies to house built, acquired, or remodeled by the 100% disabled veteran or their immediate family

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.land_limit: 1,000 square meters in urban zone or 1 cuerda (3,930 sq meters) in rural zone
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.land_note: The 'cuerda' is a traditional Puerto Rican unit of land area: 1 cuerda ≈ 3,930 sq meters ≈ 4,700 sq yards ≈ 0.971 acres
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.retroactive_note: Applications for exemption may be retroactive for up to 3 years once approved
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.administering_agency: CRIM (Centro de Recaudacion de Ingresos Municipales)

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from motor vehicle taxes, license plate fees, and driver's license fees for 100% disabled veterans

eligibility.note: Must be declared 100% disabled by the VA, or vehicle must be exempt under the PR Internal Revenue Code disability provisions

va_funded_vehicle.description: Vehicles provided by the VA are fully exempt from vehicle taxes and license fees
va_funded_vehicle.replacement_rule: Replacement vehicle qualifies for exemption if the replaced vehicle was owned for at least 4 years for personal use
va_funded_vehicle.fortuitous_loss_exception: If vehicle becomes unusable due to accident/disaster not caused by owner's negligence, the 4-year rule is waived

age_based_exemption.description: Veterans over 60 are exempt from vehicle taxes and fees for license plates or driver's licenses, regardless of disability status

vehicle_tax_exemption.survivor_note: Vehicle exemption tied to the qualifying veteran; transfer provisions unclear

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Veterans with service-connected disabilities exempt from $5,000 of primary residence appraised value (up to 1,000 sq meters urban or 1 cuerda rural).
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $5,000 assessed value exemption for veterans with service-connected disabilities on primary residence.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civilian pensions taxable, but taxpayers at full retirement age with AGI below threshold may exclude up to $20,000 of pension/annuity income.

military_retirement.note: Military retirement pay fully exempt from Rhode Island income tax (effective January 1, 2023, per R.I. Gen. Laws §44-30-12(c)(11)). Taxpayers subtract the full amount of military service pension from federal AGI.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Rhode Island exempts Social Security benefits for retirees who have reached full retirement age and meet income thresholds. Higher-income filers owe state tax on a portion of benefits. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

sbp_annuities.sbp_note: SBP/RCSBP/RSFPP annuities are taxable in Rhode Island, though surviving spouses may qualify for up to $20,000 pension/annuity modification if at full retirement age and below AGI threshold

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Rhode Island veteran-specific benefits. Property tax exemptions are municipality-administered with amounts varying significantly by city/town. State law provides framework but local ordinances set specific amounts. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Rhode Island state law (RIGL 44-3-4) authorizes property tax exemptions for wartime veterans, with amounts set by each city and town. Totally disabled service-connected veterans receive enhanced exemptions. Amounts vary widely: some towns provide modest credits ($225-$614), while others provide valuation reductions ($10,000+). Contact local tax assessor for specific amounts.

eligibility.wartime_note: Service during WWII, Korea, Vietnam, or recognized post-Vietnam conflicts. See RIGL 44-3-4 and 44-3-4.2 for qualifying periods.

totally_disabled_veteran_enhanced.description: Veterans determined totally disabled through service-connected disability may receive additional $10,000 exemption from local taxation on real and personal property (per RIGL 44-3-4(c)), plus municipality-specific enhanced exemptions. Some towns provide significantly larger reductions for 100% service-connected disabled veterans.
totally_disabled_veteran_enhanced.specially_adapted_housing: $10,000 exemption for veterans who received VA specially adapted housing assistance (RIGL 44-3-4(b))

application.where: Local city or town tax assessor
documentation: VA disability rating letter (for disability-based exemptions)
application.deadline_note: Varies by municipality. Contact local assessor for filing deadlines.

veteran_property_tax_exemption.motor_vehicle_note: Totally disabled veterans or their surviving spouses may be exempt from property tax on up to two vehicles (RIGL 44-3-4)
veteran_property_tax_exemption.exemption_type_note: Classified as partial because amounts vary widely by municipality. Some towns provide modest credits while others provide significant valuation reductions. No statewide full exemption exists.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Rhode Island cities and towns administer veteran property tax exemptions independently. 100% disabled veterans may qualify for significant exemptions. Contact your local tax assessor.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Locally administered exemptions; 100% disabled veterans may qualify for significant relief. Contact local tax assessor.

military_retirement.note: South Carolina does not tax military retired pay. Full exemption effective for all retirees.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: South Carolina does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates from 0% to 6.4%
income_tax.note: South Carolina top marginal income tax rate of 6.0% (effective July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026, FY2026 budget). Previous: 6.2% (2025), 6.4% (2024).

exemptions.name: Homestead Exemption
exemptions.amount_note: First $50,000 of fair market value exempt from school operating tax for owner-occupied legal residence
exemptions.note: Available to all homeowners for the school operating millage portion

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax benefits for South Carolina. Full exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans, retroactive to 2022. Pending legislation for proportional exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from property taxes on primary residence and up to 5 acres for permanently and totally disabled veterans. Retroactive to 2022 for qualifying veterans.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.retroactive_note: Veterans applying in 2025 can claim retroactive to 2023 at the earliest (previous 2 years if taxes paid on time)

eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Verify with SC DOR — statute references permanent and total disability

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouse retains home exemption and one vehicle exemption for life or until remarriage

brain_injury_exemption.note: Veterans with specific mobility or brain injuries may be exempt from all residence taxes

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption._vehicle_cross_reference: See vehicle_tax_exemption for disabled veteran vehicle personal property tax exemption

hb_4700.would_do: Proportional exemption for veterans with less than 100% disability — percentage equal to disability rating
hb_4700.note: Would first apply to property tax years beginning after 2025

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from personal property tax on up to two private passenger vehicles for permanently and totally disabled veterans

eligibility.iu_eligible_note: Verify with SC DOR — statute references permanent and total disability
eligibility.special_license_tags: Disabled veteran special license tags from DMV under §56-3-1110 to §56-3-1130, or VA certificate of total and permanent disability
eligibility.spouse_vehicle_note: Vehicles in spouse's name qualify if spouse resides with veteran and vehicle is registered at same address

trust_owned.conditions: Vehicle titled to trustee qualifies if veteran or surviving spouse is income beneficiary of trust, satisfies all other criteria, and uses the vehicle

survivor_conditions.survivor_note: Surviving spouse retains exemption on one vehicle for life or until remarriage. Spouse who moves to SC after veteran's death also qualifies for one vehicle.

vehicle_tax_exemption.pow_moh_note: Former POWs and Medal of Honor recipients also qualify for two-vehicle exemption. MOH surviving spouse retains two vehicles; POW/Purple Heart surviving spouse retains one.

application.where: SC Department of Revenue via MyDORWAY online portal or mail
application.replacement_vehicle_note: When replacing an exempt vehicle, must redo application with DOR — transferring plates does not carry exemption

vehicle_tax_exemption.retroactive_note: Exemption can be claimed retroactive to previous 2 years if taxes paid on time within 2 years of application

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption on primary home and up to 5 acres for 100% disabled veterans (SC Code Sec. 12-37-220(B)(3)). Also includes up to 2 private passenger vehicles. Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary home and up to 5 acres plus up to 2 vehicles for 100% disabled veterans.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: South Dakota has no state income tax.

military_retirement.note: No state income tax; military retirement is not taxed

va_compensation.note: No state income tax

ss_income.note: No state income tax

income_tax.rate_note: South Dakota has no state income tax.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: South Dakota veteran-specific benefits. Partial property tax exemption for 100% P&T disabled veterans and full exemption for paraplegic veterans. No state income tax. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Exempts up to $200,000 of assessed value of owner-occupied dwelling for veterans rated permanently and totally disabled from service-connected disability. Surviving spouse exemption is $150,000. Applies to house, garage, and lot up to one acre.

eligibility.pt_note: Must be rated permanently and totally disabled from a service-connected disability by the VA

application.form: Application for Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemptions (prescribed by Secretary of Revenue)
application.where: Local County Director of Equalization (County Assessor)
application.filing_deadline: November 1
application.renewal_note: Once approved, no further applications needed unless property is sold, veteran does not occupy dwelling, or property changes use

survivor_conditions.dic_spouse_note: Surviving spouse receiving DIC from VA also eligible for $150,000 exemption

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.confidentiality_note: Application and supporting documents are confidential

paraplegic_veteran_property_tax_exemptio.description: Full property tax exemption for paraplegic veterans (loss or loss of use of both lower extremities). Applies to house, garage, and lot up to one acre. Property must be specifically designed for wheelchair use.

eligibility.paraplegic_note: Must have loss or loss of use of both lower extremities
eligibility.wheelchair_note: Dwelling must be specifically designed for wheelchair use within the structure
eligibility.one_year_occupancy: Veteran must have owned and occupied dwelling for one full calendar year before exemption becomes effective

application.form: Application for Paraplegic Veteran Property Tax Exemptions
application.filing_deadline: November 1 annually

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption (or up to $200,000 of assessed value if property exceeds that cap) on primary residence for permanently and totally disabled veterans (SDCL Sec. 10-4-40). Effectively full for most South Dakota homes.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption (or up to $200,000 assessed value cap) on primary residence for permanently and totally disabled veterans.

income_tax.note: Tennessee has no state income tax on wages or retirement income.

exemptions.name: Tax Relief for Elderly and Disabled
exemptions.eligibility: Age 65+ or permanently and totally disabled
exemptions.income_limit_note: Income limits apply — vary by program year
exemptions.note: State reimbursement program, not a true exemption. Homeowner pays taxes, then receives state refund.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax relief for Tennessee. No state income tax. Property tax relief is a reimbursement program — not an exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_relief.description: State reimbursement of property taxes on primary residence for qualifying disabled veterans. Veteran pays taxes, then receives state refund. Maximum market value for tax relief calculation is $175,000.
disabled_veteran_property_tax_relief.reimbursement_note: This is a reimbursement (pay first, get refunded), not a true exemption. First year requires full payment; subsequent years may use voucher.

criteria: Permanent and total service-connected disability (100% P&T)
Permanent paralysis of both legs and lower body (paraplegia)
Loss or loss of use of two or more limbs
100% total and permanent disability from being a POW

application.note: Contact county trustee for application process and deadlines

survivor_conditions.note: Surviving spouse may qualify for continued property tax relief

disabled_veteran_property_tax_relief.credit_note: Operates as a state reimbursement — veteran pays taxes first, then receives refund

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans (TCA Sec. 67-5-704). Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% disabled veterans.

income_tax.note: Texas has no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Residence Homestead Exemption
exemptions.school_note: School district exemption of $100,000 for all homeowners (increased effective 2023)
exemptions.county_city_exemption_note: Counties and cities may offer additional exemptions up to 20% of appraised value

exemptions.name: Senior Property Tax Freeze
exemptions.note: Homeowners age 65+ can freeze their school district property tax at current level. Other taxing entities may also offer freeze.

exemptions.name: Over-65 or Disabled Exemption
exemptions.note: Additional $10,000 school tax exemption for homeowners 65+ or disabled

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax exemptions for Texas. All require documentation from VA. Texas property tax is locally assessed and administered by county appraisal districts. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

total_disability_exemption.description: Total exemption of appraised value of residence homestead for 100% disabled or IU veterans

eligibility.pt_note: 100% rating OR IU determination qualifies — P&T not specifically required
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving 100% compensation due to Individual Unemployability (IU) determination qualify
eligibility.compensation_note: Must be receiving 100% disability compensation from VA

total_disability_exemption.scope: Residence homestead only — other properties may qualify for partial exemption under § 11.22
total_disability_exemption.applies_note: Exemption removes entire appraised value from school, county, city, and special district taxes

application.file_with: County Appraisal District where property is located
application.deadline: April 30, but late applications accepted up to 5 years after delinquency date

total_disability_exemption.effective_mid_year_note: If qualifying mid-year, exemption applies immediately for applicable portion of that tax year
total_disability_exemption.retroactive_note: May receive exemption for prior tax year if qualified then

required: VA award letter showing 100% disability compensation
VA rating showing 100% disability or IU determination
documentation.alternative: VA Tax Letter or DPS driver's license with disability designation

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: Surviving spouse may transfer to new homestead but exemption amount capped at dollar amount from last year exemption was received on prior homestead

partial_disability_exemption.description: Partial exemption based on disability rating for veterans with 10-90% service-connected disability

eligibility.rating_note: Any service-connected disability rating from 10% to less than 100%

partial_disability_exemption.scope: Can apply to ANY one property veteran owns — not limited to homestead

additional_exemption_65_plus.eligibility: Age 65+ with 10%+ disability, OR totally blind in one or both eyes, OR lost use of one or more limbs

application.deadline: April 30, but late applications accepted up to 5 years after delinquency date

survivor_conditions.children_note: Surviving child must be under 18 and unmarried

donated_residence_exemption.description: Exemption for residence homestead donated to disabled veteran by charitable organization

eligibility.rating_note: Exemption percentage equals veteran's disability rating

donated_residence_exemption.scope: Applies to residence homestead donated by qualifying charitable organization

survivor_conditions.spouse_note: Surviving spouse receives 100% exemption if veteran had 100% rating, or same percentage as veteran otherwise

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Total property tax exemption on residence homestead for unremarried surviving spouse of service member killed in line of duty

eligibility.condition: Service member killed in the line of duty while serving in U.S. Armed Forces

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.
veteran_benefits._vehicle_note: Texas offers disabled veteran license plates and related vehicle registration fee exemptions — see Texas DMV for details.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption of residence homestead for 100% disabled or IU veterans (TX Tax Code Sec. 11.131). Surviving spouse retains and may transfer exemption to a new homestead. No remarriage restriction on surviving spouse.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full exemption of residence homestead for 100% disabled or IU veterans. Surviving spouse may transfer exemption.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal pensions are taxable but eligible for Retirement Tax Credit (code AO/18)

military_retirement.credit_note: Nonrefundable credit equal to 4.5% (the state's flat tax rate) of military retirement pay, effectively offsetting 100% of Utah income tax on that income
military_retirement.effectively_full_note: While technically a credit rather than an exemption, the credit rate equals the tax rate, so the net effect is zero state tax on military retirement pay
military_retirement.sbp_note: Same 4.5% credit applies to Survivor Benefit Plan payments
military_retirement.note: Utah provides a nonrefundable Military Retirement Credit (TC-40A code AJ) equal to 4.5% of military retirement pay, which equals the state's flat income tax rate. Net effect: $0 state tax on military retirement. Technically a credit, not an exemption.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.ss_note: Utah provides income-based Social Security Benefits Credit; phase-out begins at $54,000 single / $90,000 MFJ (2025)
ss_income.note: Utah taxes all income at flat 4.5% but provides a nonrefundable tax credit for Social Security benefits. Full credit for single filers with AGI below $45,000 / joint below $75,000. Credit phases out at 25 cents per dollar above thresholds. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

sbp_annuities.exempt_note: Effectively exempt via nonrefundable credit equal to 4.5% of survivor benefits
sbp_annuities.note: Same 4.5% credit mechanism as military retirement pay

income_tax.rate_note: Flat 4.5% rate (reduced from 4.55% by HB 106, 2025 General Session)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Utah veteran-specific benefits. Property tax exemption for disabled veterans varies by municipality. Income tax effectively exempt via credit mechanism. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Disabled veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability may receive property tax reduction on primary residence. Amount varies by municipality, up to $266,670 reduction based on disability level and income. Statewide minimum applies but local jurisdictions may set higher amounts.

eligibility.iu_note: Contact municipal assessor to verify IU eligibility under local rules
eligibility.income_limits_note: Income limits and exemption amounts vary by municipality

application.filing_deadline: September 1 (varies by county)

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.note: Amounts and income limits are municipality-specific. Statewide statute sets minimums but local jurisdictions administer.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: 2025: Up to $521,620 assessed value exemption for 100% disabled veterans (scales with rating for 10%+). Effectively full exemption for most Utah homes at 100% rating.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Up to $521,620 assessed value exemption for 100% disabled veterans (2025, scales with rating for 10%+).

age_deduction.filing_status_note: Per taxpayer — both spouses may each claim if both qualify

military_retirement.age_note: No age requirement (age 55 requirement removed effective 2023 tax year)
military_retirement.tsp_note: TSP distributions do NOT qualify for the military benefits subtraction
military_retirement.medal_of_honor: Full exemption (100% subtraction) for Medal of Honor recipients
military_retirement.surviving_spouse_note: Surviving spouses do NOT qualify for the military benefits subtraction
military_retirement.authority: Va. Code § 58.1-322.03(18)(b), enacted via HB30 Conference Report 2022

pending_legislation.proposal: Remove $40,000 cap entirely for tax year 2026+
pending_legislation.status: HB tabled by House Finance subcommittee Jan 2026; SB continued to 2027

military_retirement.note: Virginia allows a subtraction of up to $40,000 of military retirement pay from state taxable income (2025+). Phase-in: $10K (2022), $20K (2023), $30K (2024), $40K (2025+). No age requirement. TSP and CSRS/FERS do not qualify. SBP payments eligible. Surviving spouses not eligible.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and not taxable at state level

ss_income.note: Virginia does not tax Social Security benefits

exemptions.name: Senior and Disabled Exemption
exemptions.income_limit_note: Varies by locality — set by each county/city
exemptions.note: Virginia localities (counties/cities) administer their own senior property tax relief programs within state guidelines. Actual benefit varies significantly by location.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for Virginia. All require documentation from VA (Summary of Benefits Letter). Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section. Rules vary significantly by state.

real_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from local real estate tax on primary residence and up to one acre of land

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans rated less than 100% but compensated at 100% via Individual Unemployability (IU), AND rated P&T, also qualify

real_property_tax_exemption.acreage_note: If locality provides exemption/deferral of more than 1 acre under Article 2 (§ 58.1-3210 et seq.), the same acreage applies to this exemption
real_property_tax_exemption.manufactured_home_note: Manufactured homes qualify even if veteran does not own the land, but the land itself is not exempt in that case

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: Surviving spouse may move to a different Virginia residence and retain exemption (HB1655, 2019). Applies without restriction.

vehicle_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from personal property tax on one passenger car, pickup, or panel truck for personal use

eligibility.iu_note: Veterans compensated at 100% via IU and rated P&T qualify per VA DVS and locality administration

vehicle_tax_exemption.spouse_during_life_note: Vehicle owned by married couple qualifies if either spouse is the qualifying veteran
vehicle_tax_exemption.survivor_note: Exemption expires on date of veteran's death. Passing the exemption to a surviving spouse is prohibited by statute (§ 58.1-3668(C)).
vehicle_tax_exemption.reduced_rate_note: Localities may separately offer a reduced personal property tax rate for veterans with certain disabilities (lost limbs, blind, P&T) under § 58.1-3506(A)(19). This is independent of the full exemption and rates vary by locality.

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.description: Real property tax exemption for surviving spouse of service member who died in the line of duty (DoD Line of Duty determination)
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.scope: Separate from the 100% P&T disabled veteran exemption — this covers surviving spouses of service members who died in the line of duty, not just killed in action

eligibility.condition: Service member died in the line of duty with a Line of Duty determination from the U.S. Department of Defense

line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.effective_note: Virginia voters ratified constitutional amendment November 5, 2024, effective January 1, 2025. Expanded prior 2014 amendment from 'killed in action' to 'died in the line of duty'.
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.prior_version: Original 2014 amendment covered only 'killed in action' as determined by DoD
line_of_duty_survivor_property_tax.retroactive_note: Applies regardless of whether death occurred before the effective date, but exemption not applicable to any period before the effective date

survivor_conditions.spouse_portable_note: No restriction on spouse moving to a different principal place of residence. No requirement that spouse resided in Virginia at time of death.

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.
veteran_benefits._mcc_note: Virginia Housing (VHDA) Mortgage Credit Certificate is NOT a veteran-specific benefit. It is a general first-time homebuyer program. If modeled, it belongs in a separate homebuyer_programs section.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full exemption from local real estate taxes on primary residence (+up to 1 acre) for 100% P&T or IU veterans (VA Code Sec. 58.1-3219.5). Surviving unremarried spouse retains and may transfer to a new Virginia residence (effective July 1, 2019).
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full exemption from local real estate taxes on primary residence (+1 acre) for 100% P&T or IU veterans. Surviving spouse may transfer.

states._territory_note: Unincorporated US territory using a mirror tax code (VITIT). Residents file with the USVI Bureau of Internal Revenue at federal income tax rates, not with the IRS. The USVI offers the most territory-level veteran benefits (26) among the five territories.

income_tax._mirror_note: USVI mirrors the federal IRC. Residents pay taxes at federal rates to the USVI Bureau of Internal Revenue, not the IRS. The effective result is equivalent to living in a state with no state income tax.

military_retirement.note: Military retired pay is taxable in the USVI at federal rates

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation is federally exempt and excluded from USVI taxable income

dic.note: DIC is tax-free under both federal law and USVI mirror code

sbp.note: Survivor Benefit Plan annuities are taxable in the USVI at federal rates

disability_retirement_pay.note: Military disability retirement pay for service-connected injury/sickness is excluded from taxable income per IRS Publication 525

ss_income.note: Social Security benefits taxed under the same rules as federal — partially taxable based on combined income thresholds

income_tax.note: USVI mirrors federal tax rates. Top marginal rate is 37% (same as federal). Residents file with USVI Bureau of Internal Revenue, not IRS. Effectively equivalent to states with no state income tax.

exemptions.name: Veteran Homestead Tax Credit
exemptions.amount_note: Maximum $650 annual tax credit on primary residence
exemptions.eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans, 20+ year National Guard retirees, Guard members deployed 30+ days to combat zone, and surviving spouses
exemptions.note: Property owner of record as of January 1 of the taxable year. Application deadline is prior to March 1.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property and housing benefits for USVI. All require documentation from VA. USVI offers 26 territory-level veteran benefits per CNAS analysis — the most among all territories.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Full exemption from all property taxes for permanently and totally disabled veterans

eligibility.ownership_note: Must be property owner of record as of January 1 of the taxable year

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.administering_agency: Tax Assessors Office (separate offices for St. Croix and St. Thomas/St. John)

contact.st_croix: 1131 King Street, Suite 101, Christiansted, St. Croix, VI 00820

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.survivor_note: Surviving spouses qualify for the $650 veteran homestead credit but not the full disabled veteran exemption unless independently eligible

veteran_benefits._vehicle_note: No specific veteran vehicle personal property tax exemption identified for the USVI. The USVI does not appear to levy an annual vehicle personal property tax. Veterans should contact the USVI Bureau of Internal Revenue for current vehicle fee exemptions.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: U.S. Virgin Islands: Veteran homestead tax credit available. Contact Virgin Islands Bureau of Internal Revenue (VIBIR) for current eligibility and amounts.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Veteran homestead tax credit available. Contact VIBIR for current eligibility and amounts.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civilian pensions generally taxable in Vermont.

military_retirement.exempt_note: Income-based tiered exemption (Act 71, effective 2025 tax year)
military_retirement.note: Act 71 (signed June 25, 2025): Full exemption for taxpayers with federal AGI of $125,000 or less. Partial exemption for AGI between $125,000 and $175,000 (calculated as ($175,000 - AGI) / $50,000 × retirement income). No exemption above $175,000 AGI. Covers USPHS and NOAA uniformed retirees. Effective 2025 tax year.

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Vermont exempts Social Security income for retirees below AGI thresholds. 2026: Full exemption if AGI ≤ $55,000 (single) / $70,000 (joint). Partial exemption for AGI up to $64,999 (single) / $79,999 (joint). No exemption above those levels. One of 8 states still taxing SS in 2026.

sbp_annuities.sbp_note: Same tiered exemption as military retirement under Act 71: full exemption for AGI ≤ $125,000, partial for $125,000-$175,000, none for AGI ≥ $175,000

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates up to 8.75% on income over $229,500 (single) / $279,450 (joint). Among higher state income tax rates nationally.

low_income_veteran_credit.note: Act 71 created a $250 refundable tax credit for Vermont resident veterans with AGI ≤ $25,000. Credit phases out between $25,000 and $30,000 AGI. Effective 2025 tax year.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Vermont veteran-specific benefits. Property tax exemption for disabled veterans reduces appraised value by $10,000 (state minimum) to $40,000 (municipal option). Not a full exemption. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Property tax exemption reduces assessed value of primary residence by $10,000 (state-mandated minimum) to $40,000 (if town votes to increase). Applies to both municipal and education grand list. Not a full exemption — reduces taxable value only.

exemption_range.note: State law mandates $10,000 minimum. Towns may vote to increase up to $40,000 in the municipal grand list. Amount varies by municipality.

eligibility.rating_note: 50% or higher service-connected disability compensation from VA, OR receiving VA non-service-connected disability pension, OR permanent medical retirement from military

documentation: VA Benefits Summary Letter showing disability rating
application.renewal_note: Veterans with 50%+ disability for 10+ consecutive years, permanent medical retirees, and surviving spouses only need to apply once with VA documentation. Others apply annually.

survivor_conditions.note: Unremarried surviving spouse or minor children of veterans who were eligible at time of death

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Base state exemption of $10,000 assessed value; municipalities may increase to maximum $40,000. Exemption amounts vary significantly by town.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Base $10,000 assessed value exemption; municipalities may increase up to $40,000. Amounts vary by town.

income_tax.note: Washington has no state personal income tax

exemptions.name: Senior/Disabled Exemption
exemptions.note: Property tax exemption for seniors 61+ or disabled persons based on income thresholds

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Veteran-specific property tax relief for Washington State. Benefits are income-based with county-specific thresholds. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.description: Income-based property tax reduction for disabled veterans — amount varies by income level, property value, and local levy rates
disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.benefit_type: Value frozen and exemption from excess/special levies; possible reduction in regular levies depending on income

eligibility.rating_note: 80%+ service-connected disability OR receiving VA compensation at 100% rate
eligibility.iu_note: Veterans receiving compensation at 100% rate qualify regardless of actual disability percentage
eligibility.age_note: Veterans 61+ may also qualify under senior exemption rules

income_requirements.threshold_note: Income Threshold 3 is based on county median household income — varies by county
income_requirements.va_benefits_note: VA disability compensation and DIC are NOT counted toward combined disposable income

benefit_levels.note: Three exemption levels based on final calculated combined disposable income
benefit_levels.level_1: Highest income within threshold — frozen value, exempt from excess levies
benefit_levels.level_3: Lowest income — maximum exemptions from regular and excess levies

application.file_with: County Assessor where property is located
documentation: Application form from county assessor or WA DOR website
VA Benefit Summary Letter or DOR Proof of Disability Statement
application.renewal: Required at least once every 6 years; county will send renewal notice

disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.scope: Primary residence and up to one acre of land; mobile homes may qualify even if land is leased
disabled_veteran_property_tax_reduction.survivor_transfer_note: Veteran exemption does not automatically transfer; surviving spouses may qualify for separate grant program

pending_legislation.proposed_change: Would provide full property tax exemption for 100% disabled or IU veterans, plus tiered exemptions for lower ratings ($5K-$12K based on rating)

surviving_spouse_grant_program.description: Grant assistance for property tax payment for qualifying surviving spouses of veterans
surviving_spouse_grant_program.benefit_type: Grant to help pay property taxes — does not need to be repaid if residence requirements met

eligibility.veteran_condition: Veteran had 100% disability rating or died from service-connected causes

income_requirements.income_note: Combined disposable income must be $40,000 or less (VA benefits excluded)

repayment.condition: Must continue living in residence until at least December 15 of year grant received

application.contact_alternative: WA Department of Revenue Grant Administrator at (360) 534-1400

veteran_benefits._funding_fee_note: VA funding fee exemption is a federal benefit. See federal_veteran_benefits at root level of this file.

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T or IU disabled veterans (RCW 84.36.379). Surviving unremarried spouse retains.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for 100% P&T or IU disabled veterans.

military_retirement.note: Wisconsin fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: Wisconsin does not tax Social Security benefits

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 7.65% on income over $405,550 (2025)

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Wisconsin veteran-specific benefits. Property tax relief delivered via refundable income tax credit, not exemption. 100% of property taxes reimbursed for qualifying veterans. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_property_tax_credit.description: Refundable income tax credit equal to 100% of property taxes paid on primary residence. Not a traditional property tax exemption — filed on Wisconsin income tax return after WDVA certification.

eligibility.iu_note: Certain IU cases qualify; WDVA certification required
eligibility.residency_requirement: Must have been Wisconsin resident upon entry into military service or for five consecutive years after beginning service
eligibility.land_limit: Primary residence including land up to one acre

application.where: Wisconsin Department of Veterans Affairs (WDVA) for eligibility certification; claim on Wisconsin income tax return
application.reapplication: Annual claim on income tax return

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for veterans with 100% service-connected disability (Wis. Stat. Sec. 74.35). Surviving spouse may also qualify.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on primary residence for veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Federal civilian pensions are generally taxable in West Virginia

military_retirement.note: West Virginia fully exempts military retirement pay from state income tax

va_compensation.note: VA disability compensation conforms to federal exemption

ss_income.note: West Virginia exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax

sbp_annuities.note: SBP annuities are exempt from West Virginia state income tax

income_tax.rate_note: Graduated rates; top rate 4.65% (2025, rates have been reducing). Standard deduction mirrors federal amounts.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: West Virginia veteran-specific benefits. Property tax credit for 90-100% disabled veterans (2024+); 100% P&T may qualify for full exemption under §11-6B. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

disabled_veteran_real_property_tax_credi.description: Disabled Veteran Real Property Tax Credit: honorably discharged veterans with 90-100% combined VA disability rating may claim income tax credit equal to property taxes paid on primary residence. Effective beginning tax year 2024.

eligibility.iu_note: Statute specifies 90-100% combined disability rating; IU eligibility not explicitly addressed
eligibility.timely_payment_note: Property taxes must be paid on time (first half by Oct 1, second half by April 1)

application.where: Filed with West Virginia income tax return
application.filing_deadline: Filed with annual income tax return

disabled_veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Veterans rated 100% permanent and totally disabled by the VA may be exempt from certain property taxes under §11-6B

eligibility.iu_note: Statute references 100% permanent and total disability; contact county assessor for IU eligibility

application.where: County Assessor's Office or local Department of Veterans Assistance

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Full property tax exemption on homestead for 100% P&T disabled veterans (WV Code Sec. 11-6F-2). Surviving unremarried spouse may qualify.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: Full property tax exemption on homestead for 100% P&T disabled veterans.

income_tax.taxes_federal_pension_note: Wyoming has no state income tax.

military_retirement.note: No state income tax; military retirement is not taxed

va_compensation.note: No state income tax

ss_income.note: No state income tax

income_tax.rate_note: Wyoming has no state income tax.

veteran_benefits._schema_note: Wyoming veteran-specific benefits. Modest $6,000 assessed value property tax exemption for qualifying veterans (not disability-specific for 100% P&T). No state income tax. Federal benefits (funding fee) are in the federal_veteran_benefits root section.

veteran_property_tax_exemption.description: Property tax exemption of $6,000 in assessed value on primary residence (increased from $3,000, effective 2025). Approximate tax savings of $350-$400 depending on local mill levy. May alternatively be applied to vehicle licensing fees (~$180). Available to wartime veterans, expeditionary medal recipients, and disabled veterans with any compensable service-connected disability.
veteran_property_tax_exemption.exemption_note: $6,000 assessed value reduction (effective 2025, doubled from prior $3,000). Not specific to 100% disability — any compensable service-connected disability qualifies.

qualifying_criteria: Honorably discharged Korean War veteran (Jun 25, 1950 - Jan 31, 1955)
Honorably discharged Vietnam War veteran (Feb 28, 1961 - May 7, 1975)
Honorably discharged veteran awarded Armed Forces Expeditionary Medal or equivalent campaign medal
Disabled veteran with any compensable service-connected disability certified by VA
eligibility.residency_requirement: Must be Wyoming resident for 3 or more consecutive years

application.filing_deadline: Fourth Monday in May (annually)
documentation: VA certification of disability (for disability-based qualification)

vehicle_alternative.description: If exemption not used on real property, may be applied to vehicle licensing fees. Approximately $180 annual reduction. Multiple vehicles may qualify up to $180 total.
vehicle_alternative.note: Vehicle must be titled in veteran's or spouse's name. Leased vehicles do not qualify.

survivor_conditions.spouse_note: Surviving spouse must have been Wyoming resident at time of veteran's death and not remarried

disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.note: Wyoming: ~$3,000 assessed value reduction for 100% disabled veterans. Low benefit relative to most home values. Contact your county assessor.
disabled_veteran_100pct_property_tax.description: $3,000 assessed value reduction for 100% disabled veterans. Low benefit relative to most Wyoming home values.

_expansion_note: Coverage: 50 states + DC + 5 US territories (56 entries). Territory expansion added in v2.1: PR, GU, VI, CNMI (MP), and American Samoa (AS). Territories have distinct tax systems — see is_territory flag and _territory_note.

federal_veteran_benefits._schema_note: Federal veteran benefits that apply identically in all states. Included at root level to avoid repeating in every state entry. State entries reference this section.

va_funding_fee_exemption.description: Waiver of one-time VA home loan funding fee on purchase, refinance, and construction loans
va_funding_fee_exemption.fee_range: 0.5% to 3.3% of loan amount depending on loan type, down payment, and prior use
va_funding_fee_exemption.fee_range_note: Current rates effective through 2031 per Blue Water Navy Vietnam Veterans Act of 2019

conditions: Receiving VA compensation for service-connected disability (any rating)
Eligible for VA compensation but receiving retirement or active-duty pay instead
Active-duty service member with Purple Heart (on or before loan closing date)
Service member with proposed or memorandum rating from pre-discharge claim before closing

va_funding_fee_exemption.tax_deductible_note: Starting 2026, VA funding fees are deductible on federal tax returns
va_funding_fee_exemption.retroactive_note: If disability rating approved retroactive to before loan closing, veteran may claim funding fee refund via VA RLC

age_57_rule.description: Remarriage after age 57 does not bar housing loan benefits (including funding fee exemption)

age_55_rule.description: Remarriage after age 55 does not bar DIC or CHAMPVA benefits, but housing loan benefits require age 57+

terminated_remarriage.description: If remarriage ends by death, divorce, or annulment, DIC and related benefits may be reinstated regardless of age at remarriage
